Aberrant Form.entries.0: Your creature type becomes Aberration in addition to any other creature types you have.
Aberrant Form.entries.1: Also, the first time you become {@condition Bloodied|XPHB} after a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you gain a number of {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} plus your current Transformation Stage.
Aberrant Form.name: Aberrant Form
Aberrant Mutation.entries.0: Your body can twist and reshape itself as you will it, changing body parts into dangerous or useful tools and regenerating after taking damage. These abilities are represented by Aberrant Mutations. You can manifest an Aberrant Mutation listed below a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} plus your current Transformation Stage.
Aberrant Mutation.entries.1: Each of these Aberrant Mutations last for 1 minute, and you can end them early by spending a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to remove them or take on a different mutation. If you choose another Aberrant Mutation, you must spend another use of this feature.
Aberrant Mutation.entries.2: You regain all uses of this ability when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Aberrant Mutation.entries.3.entries.0: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you grow a hard, crustacean-like shell. While this mutation is active and are not wearing heavy armor, your {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB} increases by 2. While you maintain this shell, your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is reduced by 10 feet.
Aberrant Mutation.entries.3.name: Chitinous Shell
Aberrant Mutation.entries.4.entries.0: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you transform one or both of your arms into thick muscle, scything claws, or sharpened bone. When you use the {@action Attack|XPHB} action, you can replace or or more attacks with melee attacks made with your eldritch limb. You are considered proficient with this attack, and it uses either Strength or Dexterity (your choice). On a hit, the attack deals {@damage 1d8} damage (either Bludgeoning, Piercing, or Slashing, chosen each time you manifest the mutation).
Aberrant Mutation.entries.4.entries.1: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can make a melee attack with your eldritch limb.
Aberrant Mutation.entries.4.entries.2: Your eldritch limbs cannot hold weapons, shields, or other items. They are not considered weapons or {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB|Unarmed Strikes}.
Aberrant Mutation.entries.4.name: Eldritch Limbs
Aberrant Mutation.entries.5.entries.0: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you cover yourself in a slippery slime. You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on ability checks to escape a grapple, and you can use the {@action Dash|XPHB} action as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. You also gain {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Acid, Fire, and Cold damage while in this form.
Aberrant Mutation.entries.5.name: Slimy Form
Aberrant Mutation.name: Aberrant Mutation
Abyssal Resistance.entries.0: You gain {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to the damage type chosen for {@optfeature Fiendish Soul|GrimHollowPG24}, and you gain {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to the other two damage types. Additionally, you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to damage from nonmagical weapon attacks or {@variantrule Unarmed Strikes|XPHB}.
Abyssal Resistance.name: Abyssal Resistance
Acid Blood (Aberration, Ooze, Plant).ent: For 1 minute, your blood becomes acidic. Each time a creature hits you with an attack while within 5 feet of you, it takes {@damage 2d6} Acid damage.
Acolyte of Undeath.entries.0: When you reduce a Humanoid to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} and it dies, you can use a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to immediately transform it into a {@creature Zombie|XMM} under your control. You can have three such creatures under your control at once. These Zombies act on their own {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} count, following your verbal commands to the best of their ability.
Acolyte of Undeath.name: Acolyte of Undeath
Acumen (Celestial, Fiend, Fey).entries.0: For 1 hour, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Wisdom ({@skill Insight|XPHB}) checks. Additionally, choose either {@skill Intimidation|XPHB} or {@skill Persuasion|XPHB}. You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on ability checks using the chosen skill.
Acumen (Celestial, Fiend, Fey).name: Acumen (Celestial, Fiend, Fey)
Adept of the Green Sisterhood.entries.0: As your connection to your Green Sisterhood grows, your body and your magical skills strengthen.
You gain a Claw attack that deals {@dice 1d6} plus your Strength or Dexterity modifier Slashing Damage. You are proficient with this attack, and you add either your Strength or Dexterity modifier to attack and damage rolls. This attack is not an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, nor can it be used in place of an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}.

You can hide yourself from others. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can cast {@spell Invisibility|XPHB} without expending a spell slot. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Transformation Stage. You regain all uses after finishing a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Adept of the Green Sisterhood.name: Adept of the Green Sisterhood
Adept of the Red Sisterhood.entries.0: As your connection to your Red Sisterhood grows, your body and your magical skills strengthen.
Adept of the Red Sisterhood.entries.1.it: You gain a Claw attack that deals {@dice 1d6} plus your Strength or Dexterity modifier Slashing Damage. You are proficient with this attack, and you add either your Strength or Dexterity modifier to attack and damage rolls. This attack is not an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, nor can it be used in place of an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}.

You can sway others to your will. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can cast {@spell Charm Person|XPHB} without expending a spell slot. The spell save DC when cast in this way is 12 plus your Transformation Stage. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Transformation Stage. You regain all uses after finishing a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Adept of the Red Sisterhood.name: Adept of the Red Sisterhood
Adept of the Sea Sisterhood.entries.0: As your connection to your Sea Sisterhood grows, your body and your magical skills strengthen.
Adept of the Sea Sisterhood.entries.1.it: You gain a Claw attack that deals {@dice 1d6} plus your Strength or Dexterity modifier Slashing Damage. You are proficient with this attack, and you add either your Strength or Dexterity modifier to attack and damage rolls. This attack is not an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, nor can it be used in place of an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}.

You can appear as someone other than yourself. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can cast {@spell Disguise Self|XPHB} without expending a spell slot. The spell save DC when cast in this way is 12 plus your Transformation Stage. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Transformation Stage. You regain all uses after finishing a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Adept of the Sea Sisterhood.name: Adept of the Sea Sisterhood
Adrenal Injection.entries.0: For 1 minute, your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases by 10 feet. In addition, once on each of your turns, you can jump up to 30 feet by spending 10 feet of movement.
Adrenal Injection.name: Adrenal Injection
Agonizing Blast.entries.0: Choose one of your known {@filter Warlock cantrips|spells|level=0|class=Warlock|damage type=acid;bludgeoning;cold;fire;force;lightning;necrotic;piercing;poison;psychic;radiant;slashing;thunder} that deals damage. You can add your Charisma modifier to that spell's damage rolls.
Agonizing Blast.entries.1.entries.0.entr: You can gain this invocation more than once. Each time you do so, choose a different eligible cantrip.
Agonizing Blast.entries.1.entries.0.name: Repeatable
Agonizing Blast.name: Agonizing Blast
Allsense Injection.entries.0: For 1 minute, you have {@sense Blindsight|XPHB} with a range of 30 feet.
Allsense Injection.name: Allsense Injection
Ambush.entries.0: When you make a Dexterity ({@skill Stealth|XPHB}) check or an {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} roll, you can expend one Superiority Die and add the die to the roll, unless you have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition.
Ambush.name: Ambush
Amphibious.entries.0: Surviving underwater is second nature to you. You can breathe air and water.
Amphibious.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on ability checks or saving throws made while submerged in water. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Amphibious.entries.1.name: Water Born
Amphibious.name: Amphibious
Angelic Wings.entries.0: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can manifest feathered wings for 1 hour. While they are manifested, you have a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} when you are not wearing heavy armor.
Angelic Wings.entries.1: You can manifest these wings a number of times equal to your Transformation Stage, and you regain all uses after finishing a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Angelic Wings.name: Angelic Wings
Animal Friend.entries.0: Time spent among beasts has gifted you a way with those creatures. You have proficiency in the {@skill Animal Handling|XPHB} skill.
Animal Friend.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@skill Animal Handling|XPHB} checks. You can use this feature a number of times equal to twice your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Animal Friend.entries.1.name: Animal Ally
Animal Friend.name: Animal Friend
Arcane Eye Oil.entries.0: For 1 hour, you sense the presence of magical effects within 30 feet of yourself. If you sense such effects, you can take the {@action Magic|XPHB} action to see a faint aura around any visible creature or object in the area that bears the magic, and if an effect was created by a spell, you learn the spell's school of magic.
Arcane Eye Oil.entries.1: This sense is blocked by 1 foot of stone, dirt, or wood; 1 inch of metal; or a thin sheet of lead.
Arcane Eye Oil.name: Arcane Eye Oil
Arcane Propulsion Armor.entries.0: The wearer of this armor gains these benefits:
The wearer's walking speed increases by 5 feet.

The armor includes gauntlets, each of which is a magic melee weapon that can be wielded only when the hand is holding nothing. The wearer is proficient with the gauntlets, and each one deals {@damage 1d8} force damage on a hit and has the thrown property, with a normal range of 20 feet and a long range of 60 feet. When thrown, the gauntlet detaches and flies at the attack's target, then immediately returns to the wearer and reattaches.
The armor can't be removed against the wearer's will.
If the wearer is missing any limbs, the armor replaces those limbs—hands, arms, feet, legs, or similar appendages. The replacements function identically to the body parts they replace.
Arcane Propulsion Armor.name: Arcane Propulsion Armor
Arch-Crone's Hunger.entries.0: You develop a taste for strange, cursed, or vile foods, and normal food no longer nourishes you. You must consume one of these unusual foods at least once every day or you gain 1 {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} level, which cannot be removed until you feed your vile hunger. If you are forced to eat or drink normal food, you must succeed on a DC 18 Constitution saving throw or vomit it up and gain 1 {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} level.
Arch-Crone's Hunger.entries.1: Examples of vile foods include the following:
Arch-Crone's Hunger.entries.2.items.0: The flesh of sapient creatures (Humanoids, Fey, or Celestials)
Arch-Crone's Hunger.entries.2.items.1: Magical ingredients (rare herbs, dragon's blood, etc.)
Arch-Crone's Hunger.entries.2.items.2: The pain and suffering of others
Arch-Crone's Hunger.name: Arch-Crone's Hunger
Armor of Magical Strength.entries.0: This armor has 6 charges. The wearer can expend the armor's charges in the following ways:
Armor of Magical Strength.entries.1.item: When the wearer makes a Strength check or a Strength saving throw, it can expend 1 charge to add a bonus to the roll equal to its Intelligence modifier.
If the creature would be knocked {@condition prone}, it can use its reaction to expend 1 charge to avoid being knocked prone.
Armor of Magical Strength.entries.2: The armor regains {@dice 1d6} expended charges daily at dawn.
Armor of Magical Strength.name: Armor of Magical Strength
Armor of Shadows.entries.0: You can cast {@spell Mage Armor|XPHB} on yourself without expending a spell slot.
Armor of Shadows.name: Armor of Shadows
Artifice Expertise.entries.0: Working with detritus and shattered objects has granted you an affinity for repairing and remaking things. You have proficiency with {@item Tinker's Tools|XPHB}. (This is an Exploration trait.) Additionally, you can use your {@item Tinker's Tools|XPHB} and 10 GP worth of appropriate materials to spend 10 minutes creating a small clockwork device. The device must fit in the palm of your hand, and can serve one of the following functions:
Artifice Expertise.entries.1.entries.0: The device exudes smoke in a 5-foot {@variantrule Cube [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cube} for 1 minute. Any objects or creatures within this {@variantrule Cube [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cube} are considered {@variantrule Lightly Obscured|XPHB}.
Artifice Expertise.entries.1.name: {@style Smoker|small}
Artifice Expertise.entries.2.entries.0: The device emits a small flame the size of a candle's that can light flammable objects.
Artifice Expertise.entries.2.name: {@style Lighter|small}
Artifice Expertise.entries.3.entries.0: The device always points north, or in a cardinal direction of the GM's determination on another plane.
Artifice Expertise.entries.3.name: {@style Compass|small}
Artifice Expertise.entries.4.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, you can make a device in 1 minute instead of 10 minutes. In addition, you can choose to imbue a device with the following extra function: (This is a Combat trait.)
Artifice Expertise.entries.4.entries.1.e: This device is set with blinking lights that can captivate other creatures. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you place or toss the device into a space within 30 feet of you. A creature sharing a space with the device must succeed on a DC 10 Intelligence saving throw. On a failure, attacks against that creature have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} until the start of your next turn. A creature can use an action to destroy the device. You can give up to three of your devices the Distractor feature. You regain the ability to do so when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Artifice Expertise.entries.4.name: Expert Gadgeteer
Artifice Expertise.name: Artifice Expertise
Artificial Form.entries.0: You were made, not born, and your unnatural origin forever marks you as different. You are a Construct, but your enchanted form still benefits from healing spells. You can also heal yourself by spending {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB|Hit Dice} during {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short Rests} and {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB|Long Rests}, as normal.
Artificial Form.entries.1: You don't need to eat, drink, sleep, or breathe. You must still be inactive for 8 hours during a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} to gain its benefits.
Artificial Form.entries.2.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, when the {@spell Mending|XPHB} cantrip is cast on you, you can spend a {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB|Hit Die} to regain a number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the roll of the die plus your Constitution modifier (minimum 1 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point}). You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Artificial Form.entries.2.name: Self-Repair
Artificial Form.name: Artificial Form
Artisanal Focus.entries.0: You revere the crafting skill of ancestors long dead. Choose an {@item Artisan's Tools|XPHB}. You have proficiency with that tool.
Artisanal Focus.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait multiple times, you gain proficiency with a new tool each time.
Artisanal Focus.entries.1.entries.1: Additionally, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on ability checks made using any tool you selected with Artisanal Focus. You can use this feature a number of times equal to twice your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Artisanal Focus.entries.1.name: Artisanal Expertise
Artisanal Focus.name: Artisanal Focus
Ascendant Step.entries.0: You can cast {@spell Levitate|XPHB} on yourself without expending a spell slot.
Ascendant Step.name: Ascendant Step
Aspect of the Moon.entries.0: You no longer need to sleep and can't be forced to sleep by any means. To gain the benefits of a long rest, you can spend all 8 hours doing light activity, such as reading your Book of Shadows and keeping watch.
Aspect of the Moon.name: Aspect of the Moon
Athlete's Spirit.entries.0: Your reserves of physical power have kept you alive on more than one occasion. You have proficiency in the {@skill Athletics|XPHB} skill.
Athlete's Spirit.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Athletics checks. You can use this feature a number of times equal to twice your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Athlete's Spirit.entries.1.name: Athlete's Resolve
Athlete's Spirit.name: Athlete's Spirit
Aura of Awakening.entries.0: You emit an aura of power that awakens the elemental forces in your companions. When you first gain this feature, choose one of the options below. You may change the aura you emit upon finishing a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Aura of Awakening.entries.1.entries.0: Whenever you or an ally you can see within 30 feet of you makes a Dexterity saving throw, the creature gains a bonus equal to your Constitution modifier.
Aura of Awakening.entries.1.name: Light as Air
Aura of Awakening.entries.2.entries.0: When an ally within 10 feet of you deals damage with a melee attack, you can use a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to make the attack deal Fire damage instead of the normal damage type, and add {@damage 2d6} Fire damage to the attack's damage.
Aura of Awakening.entries.2.name: Forged in Fire
Aura of Awakening.entries.3.entries.0: You can choose to have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on any {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} check. If you do, as a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}, you and each creature of your choice within 30 feet of you gain {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to your {@dice 1d10} plus your Constitution modifier. While a creature has {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} gained in this manner, they cannot have the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition unless it is part of the {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} condition.
Aura of Awakening.entries.3.name: Heart of Stone
Aura of Awakening.entries.4.entries.0: When an ally within 30 feet moves or attempts to escape a Grapple, you can use a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to give them the following benefits:
Aura of Awakening.entries.4.entries.1.it: Their {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases by 10 feet until the end of their turn.
Their movement doesn't provoke {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks}.
They have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on checks to escape the Grapple.
Aura of Awakening.entries.4.name: Fluid Movement
Aura of Awakening.name: Aura of Awakening
Aura of Holy Purge.entries.0: You emit an aura of righteous fervor in a 20-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} centered on you, except when you have the {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} condition. When an ally within your aura hits with a weapon attack, the ally can use their {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to cause the hit to be a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB}. If they do, you and the ally each gain a level of {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB}. After an ally uses this {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}, that ally can't use it again until after they finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Aura of Holy Purge.name: Aura of Holy Purge
Aura of Righteous Mercy.entries.0: You emit an aura of peaceful resolution in a 20-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} centered on you, except when you have the {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} condition. When an ally within your aura would be reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, they can use their {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to drop to 1 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} instead. If they do, you and the ally each gain a level of {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB}. After an ally uses this {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}, that ally can't use it again until after they finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Aura of Righteous Mercy.name: Aura of Righteous Mercy
Awakened Mind.entries.0: The dangers of Etharis have given you a focus that allows you to shrug off debilitating magical effects. You automatically succeed on saving throws against magical effects that would give you the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB}, {@condition Stunned|XPHB}, or {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} conditions. This does not include effects that leave you {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} because you are reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}.
Awakened Mind.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, you also have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma saving throws.
Awakened Mind.entries.1.name: Reawakened
Awakened Mind.name: Awakened Mind
Awareness (All).entries.0: For 10 minutes, you know the location of creatures that are the same type as the creature you consumed within 60 feet of you that are not behind {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Total Cover}.
Awareness (All).name: Awareness (All)
Awesome Critical.entries.0: When fortune favors your blade, you know how to make it count. When you score a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} with a melee attack with a weapon or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, you can roll one of the weapon's damage dice one additional time and add it to the extra damage of the {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB}.
Awesome Critical.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, when you use Awesome Critical, you can add the maximum of the weapon's original damage dice and the extra Awesome Critical die to the extra damage of the {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB}, rather than rolling them. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Awesome Critical.entries.1.name: Maximum Critical
Awesome Critical.name: Awesome Critical
Bait and Switch.entries.0: When you're within 5 feet of a creature on your turn, you can expend one Superiority Die and switch places with that creature, provided you spend at least 5 feet of movement and the creature is willing and doesn't have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition. This movement doesn't provoke {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks}.
Bait and Switch.entries.1: Roll the Superiority Die. Until the start of your next turn, you or the other creature (your choice) gains a bonus to AC equal to the number rolled.
Bait and Switch.name: Bait and Switch
Banishing Arrow.entries.0: You use abjuration magic to try to temporarily banish your target to a harmless location in the Feywild. The creature hit by the arrow must also succeed on a Charisma saving throw or be banished. While banished in this way, the target's speed is 0, and it is {@condition incapacitated}. At the end of its next turn, the target reappears in the space it vacated or in the nearest unoccupied space if that space is occupied.
Banishing Arrow.entries.1: After you reach 18th level in this class, a target also takes {@damage 2d6} force damage when the arrow hits it.
Banishing Arrow.name: Banishing Arrow
Barbarian.entries.0: You've learned to fight with primal ferocity.
Barbarian.entries.1: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} while you aren't wearing Heavy armor, you can enter a rampage for 1 minute. During this time, you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage, and you can't maintain {@status Concentration|XPHB}. Your rampage ends early if you use a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} or have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition.
Barbarian.entries.2: Once you use this talent, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Barbarian.name: Barbarian
Battlefield Control.entries.0: When foes attempt to press you in melee, they do so at their peril. Other creatures provoke {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks} from you whenever they move into your reach, in addition to when they move out of your reach.
Battlefield Control.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks}. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Battlefield Control.entries.1.name: Battlefield Dominance
Battlefield Control.name: Battlefield Control
Beacon to Darkness.entries.0: You are now a Seraph of noted virtue and divinity, becoming a target for the world's evil. Whenever you or an ally within 30 feet commits an evil act, such as murdering innocents, you acquire a trace of darkness and you are Corrupted. GMs determine what constitutes an evil act.
Beacon to Darkness.entries.1: While you are Corrupted, whenever you make an attack roll against an evil creature or a saving throw against a spell or effect from an evil creature, you have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the check. Also, if an ally is the one who caused your Corruption, they cannot be considered your ally or you theirs until the Corruption is removed.
Beacon to Darkness.entries.2: You can remove the Corrupted condition by finishing a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, during which you must pray for at least 1 hour and burn holy incense or donate wealth to a local good cause worth at least 100 GP times your Transformation Stage.
Beacon to Darkness.name: Beacon to Darkness
Beast Speech.entries.0: You can cast {@spell speak with animals} at will, without expending a spell slot.
Beast Speech.name: Beast Speech
Beguiler's Charm.entries.0: You gain the ability to manipulate the mind of a creature with your unearthly charm. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can choose a Humanoid or Beast within 30 feet of you that can see you. The creature must succeed on a Charisma saving throw with a DC of 8 plus your Charisma modifier plus your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}.
Beguiler's Charm.entries.1: On a failed save, the creature has the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} condition for 24 hours. At the end of the 24 hours, you can choose to automatically renew the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} condition on that creature with no saving throw. You may only have one creature {@condition Charmed|XPHB} in this manner at a time. If you use this feature on another creature, or you or an ally damages the creature, the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} condition ends on the previous creature immediately.
Beguiler's Charm.name: Beguiler's Charm
Beguiling Arrow.entries.0: Your enchantment magic causes this arrow to temporarily beguile its target. The creature hit by the arrow takes an extra {@damage 2d6} psychic damage, and choose one of your allies within 30 feet of the target. The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw, or it is {@condition charmed} by the chosen ally until the start of your next turn. This effect ends early if the chosen ally attacks the {@condition charmed} target, deals damage to it, or forces it to make a saving throw.
Beguiling Arrow.entries.1: The psychic damage increases to {@damage 4d6} when you reach 18th level in this class.
Beguiling Arrow.name: Beguiling Arrow
Beguiling Influence.entries.0: You gain proficiency in the {@skill Deception} and {@skill Persuasion} skills.
Beguiling Influence.name: Beguiling Influence
Bestial Vigor.entries.0: Your {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} Maximum increases by an amount equal to your character level, and it increases by 1 every time you gain a character level.
Bestial Vigor.entries.1: Additionally, when in your hybrid form, you gain 5 {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} at the start of each of your turns.
Bestial Vigor.name: Bestial Vigor
Bewitching Whispers.entries.0: You can cast {@spell compulsion} once using a warlock spell slot. You can't do so again until you finish a long rest.
Bewitching Whispers.name: Bewitching Whispers
Binding Curse.entries.0: You can compel a creature with a binding curse. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, choose a creature within 30 feet who can see you. That creature makes a Charisma saving throw against a DC of 12 plus your Transformation Stage. On a failed save it cannot move more than 30 feet away from you for 1 minute. Also, during that time, your weapon attacks and {@variantrule Unarmed Strikes|XPHB} against the creature deal an additional {@dice 2d6} Necrotic damage.
Binding Curse.entries.1: You can use this feature three times, regaining all uses after finishing a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Binding Curse.name: Binding Curse
Bite the Bullet.entries.0: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend one {@classFeature Risk|Gunslinger|ValdaGunslinger|2|ValdaGunslinger|Risk Die} to gain {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the number rolled on the die plus your Gunslinger level.
Bite the Bullet.name: Bite the Bullet
Blindfire.entries.0: You can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} and expend one {@classFeature Risk|Gunslinger|ValdaGunslinger|2|ValdaGunslinger|Risk Die} to gain {@sense Blindsight|XPHB} with a range of 30 feet until the end of the current turn.
Blindfire.name: Blindfire
Blinding Radiance.entries.0: Your Transformation into a Seraph brings with it changes to your physical features. Your body radiates a divine glow, your eyes may become hollow braziers of celestial light, or your many sets of wings may appear unsettling to mortal creatures.
Blinding Radiance.entries.1: You can suspend this form and manifest the appearance of the humanoid you once were, but this is taxing and requires effort. This form is not permanent, and moments of stress or wrath are likely to reveal your true nature. This might occur under the following circumstances:
Blinding Radiance.entries.2.items.4: Using your Seraph Transformation powers for the first time after a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}
Blinding Radiance.entries.3: In these events, or times of other extreme emotional or physical stress, a GM can call  or a Constitution saving throw with a DC based on your current Transformation Stage.  If you fail this save, your Blinding Radiance is revealed.
Blinding Radiance.entries.4.caption: Blinding Radiance Save DC
Blinding Radiance.entries.5: When your true nature is revealed this way, all non-enemy creatures within 30 feet of you who can see you must succeed on a DC 15 Charisma saving throw or have the {@condition Blinded|XPHB} condition for 1 minute. Additionally, for the next minute, attacks against you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB}.
Blinding Radiance.name: Blinding Radiance
Bond of the Talisman.entries.0: While someone else is wearing your talisman, you can use your action to teleport to the unoccupied space closest to them, provided the two of you are on the same plane of existence. The wearer of your talisman can do the same thing, using their action to teleport to you. The teleportation can be used a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and all expended uses are restored when you finish a long rest.
Bond of the Talisman.name: Bond of the Talisman
Born Healer.entries.0: When others suffer, you are there to help. You have proficiency in the {@skill Medicine|XPHB} skill.
Born Healer.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@skill Medicine|XPHB} checks. You can use this feature a number of times equal to twice your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Born Healer.entries.1.name: Combat Doctor
Born Healer.name: Born Healer
Bow of Celestial Domination.entries.0: While your Bow of Celestial Judgement is manifested, you gain these additional benefits:
Bow of Celestial Domination.entries.1.it: When you use a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to target a creature you can see within 120 feet, you can also affect one other creature with 10 feet of the original target.
You have {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to Necrotic damage instead of {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB}.
You gain 15 {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} at the start of each of your turns instead of 5.
Bow of Celestial Domination.name: Bow of Celestial Domination
Bow of Celestial Judgement.entries.0: You can use a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to manifest a powerful bow made of divine light. The Bow of Celestial Judgement lasts for 1 minute and grants you the following abilities while manifested:
Bow of Celestial Judgement.entries.1.ite: You can use a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to target a creature you can see within 120 feet with your bow. The creature must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw against a DC equal to 8 plus your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} plus your Transformation Stage. On a failed save, the creature takes {@damage 6d6} Radiant damage, or {@dice 10d6} if it is a Fey, Fiend, or Undead. On a success, the creature takes half damage.
You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Necrotic damage.
You gain 5 {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} at the start of each of your turns.
Bow of Celestial Judgement.entries.2: You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Transformation Stage, and you regain all uses after finishing a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Bow of Celestial Judgement.name: Bow of Celestial Judgement
Brace.entries.0: When a creature you can see moves into the reach you have with the melee weapon you're wielding, you can use your reaction to expend one superiority die and make one attack against the creature, using that weapon. If the attack hits, add the superiority die to the weapon's damage roll.
Brace.name: Brace
Brave.entries.0: The horrors you've lived through have hardened you. You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws to avoid being {@condition Frightened|XPHB}.
Brave.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to bolster the spirits of your allies, granting one ally who can see or hear you {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on a saving throw against being {@condition Frightened|XPHB}. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Brave.entries.1.name: Infectious Bravery
Brave.name: Brave
Breath Weapon.entries.0: A connection to draconic or elemental fury lets you unleash a blast of destructive energy. When you select this trait, choose a damage type: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, Poison, or Thunder. Then choose an area of effect: a {@variantrule Line [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Line} that is 5 feet wide and 30 feet long, or a 15-foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone}.
Breath Weapon.entries.1: When you use a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to expel your Breath Weapon, each creature in the area of effect must make a Dexterity saving throw (DC = 8 + your Constitution modifier + your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}). A target creature takes {@damage 1d8} damage of the chosen type on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. This damage increases by {@dice 1d8} when you reach character levels 5 ({@dice 2d8}), 11 ({@dice 3d8}), and 17 ({@dice 4d8}).
Breath Weapon.entries.2: You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Breath Weapon.entries.3.entries.0: If you take this trait multiple times, you gain an additional breath weapon each time, with its own number of uses, damage type, and area of effect. Additionally, when you use any of your Breath Weapons, one target of your choice has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the saving throw. You regain the use of this feature when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Breath Weapon.entries.3.name: Potent Breath
Breath Weapon.name: Breath Weapon
Breath of Winter.entries.0: You can spend 6 ki points to cast {@spell cone of cold}.
Breath of Winter.name: Breath of Winter
Burst of Speed.entries.0: The many things that want to kill you must catch you first. On your turn, you can increase your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} by 30 feet until the end of your turn. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Burst of Speed.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, on a turn when you use Burst of Speed, you don't provoke {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks}.
Burst of Speed.entries.1.name: Furious Speed
Burst of Speed.name: Burst of Speed
Bursting Arrow.entries.0: You imbue your arrow with force energy drawn from the school of evocation. The energy detonates after your attack. Immediately after the arrow hits the creature, the target and all other creatures within 10 feet of it take {@damage 2d6} force damage each.
Bursting Arrow.entries.1: The force damage increases to {@damage 4d6} when you reach 18th level in this class.
Bursting Arrow.name: Bursting Arrow
Calculating Listener.entries.0: The weak-willed around you are easy targets for your manipulation. By conversing with a nonhostile creature for at least 1 minute, you can attempt to charm them. The creature must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw (DC = 8 + your Charisma modifier + your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}) or have the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} condition for 1 hour. At the GM's discretion, you also learn one piece of information that the target knows that relates to the topic of conversation while you speak to them. Regardless of whether or not the target succeeds on the saving throw, they remain unaware of your attempt. You regain use of this feature when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Calculating Listener.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, a creature has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the saving throw, and it has the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} condition for 8 hours on a failed save.
Calculating Listener.entries.1.name: Master Manipulator
Calculating Listener.name: Calculating Listener
Call of Unmaking.entries.0: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can give a mournful wail or otherwise set up a wracking vibration. This sound has no effect on Constructs and Undead.
Call of Unmaking.entries.1: All other creatures of your choice within 30 feet of you must make a Constitution saving throw. The DC of the Constitution saving throw is 8 plus your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} plus your Transformation Stage. Creatures that can't hear you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the saving throw.
Call of Unmaking.entries.2: On a failed save, the creature bears the Mark of Unmaking for 1 minute. While bearing this mark, a creature takes an additional {@damage 1d6} Necrotic damage each time it takes damage.
Call of Unmaking.entries.3: Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Call of Unmaking.name: Call of Unmaking
Careful Spell.entries.0: When you cast a spell that forces other creatures to make a saving throw, you can protect some of those creatures from the spell's full force. To do so, spend 1 Sorcery Point and choose a number of those creatures up to your Charisma modifier (minimum of one creature). A chosen creature automatically succeeds on its saving throw against the spell, and it takes no damage if it would normally take half damage on a successful save.
Careful Spell.name: Careful Spell
Celebrated in House.entries.0: When you request a favor from your house (as decribed under "{@optfeature Favored in House|EFA}"), it can take one of the following additional forms:
Celebrated in House.entries.1.items.0: A dragonmarked member of the house casts for you a level 3 or 4 spell associated with the house's mark at no cost.
Celebrated in House.entries.1.items.1: A member of the house casts for you a different spell of level 6 or lower at half the usual cost.
Celebrated in House.entries.1.items.2: You gain a bodyguard for the duration of your next mission for the house (maximum of 1 week). The bodyguard—a creature with a {@variantrule Challenge Rating|XPHB} no higher than half your level (round up)—is appropriate to your house.
Celebrated in House.entries.2: In addition, your house stipend increases to support your Wealthy lifestyle (4 GP per day).
Celebrated in House.name: Celebrated in House
Celestial Form.entries.0: You become a Celestial in addition to any other creature type(s) you are. Additionally, you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Radiant Damage.
Celestial Form.name: Celestial Form
Centered.entries.0: By focusing your inner strength, you gain a needed edge. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you grant yourself {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on an attack roll or ability check you make before the start of your next turn. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Centered.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, when you succeed on the attack roll or ability check made while using Centered, you can choose one creature within 30 feet of you. That creature has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the next attack roll or ability check they make before the start of your next turn.
Centered.entries.1.name: Centered Edge
Centered.name: Centered
Chains of Carceri.entries.0: You can cast {@spell hold monster} at will—targeting a celestial, fiend, or elemental—without expending a spell slot or material components. You must finish a long rest before you can use this invocation on the same creature again.
Chains of Carceri.name: Chains of Carceri
Charging Attack.entries.0: The fury with which you throw yourself into battle forces your foes to feel your wrath. If you move at least 20 feet straight toward a target and then hit it with a melee attack with a weapon or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} on the same turn, you can make another attack against the same target as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} with the same weapon.
Charging Attack.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, when you use Charging Attack, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on all attacks after the triggering movement until the end of your turn. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Charging Attack.entries.1.name: Furious Charge
Charging Attack.name: Charging Attack
Cleanse Affliction.entries.0: When you use Divine Clemency on a creature, they gain the following additional benefits:
Cleanse Affliction.entries.1.items.0: They gain {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the amount of damage healed by {@variantrule Healing|XPHB} Word.
Cleanse Affliction.entries.1.items.1: They have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on their next {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB} made within the next minute.
Cleanse Affliction.entries.1.items.2: You end one of the following conditions on it: {@condition Blinded|XPHB}, {@condition Deafened|XPHB}, {@condition Paralyzed|XPHB}, or {@condition Poisoned|XPHB}.
Cleanse Affliction.name: Cleanse Affliction
Clench of the North Wind.entries.0: You can spend 3 ki points to cast {@spell hold person}.
Clench of the North Wind.name: Clench of the North Wind
Cleric.entries.0: You've gained the ability to channel divine energy directly from the gods, emboldening allies and sanctifying their attacks. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, choose a creature within 30 feet of you. For 10 minutes, the target gains {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to half your Bard level plus your Charisma modifier. While a creature has {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} granted by this talent, it can choose to lose all remaining {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} when it hits a creature with an attack made with a weapon or {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, dealing extra Radiant damage equal to the number of {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} lost.
Cleric.entries.1: Once you use this talent, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Cleric.name: Cleric
Climber.entries.0: Sometimes staying away from what threatens you means getting clear of those threats. You have a {@variantrule Climb Speed|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}.
Climber.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, you can use your {@variantrule Climb Speed|XPHB} to move up, down, and across vertical surfaces and upside down along ceilings, while leaving your hands free.
Climber.entries.1.entries.1: Additionally, while using climbing movement, you can use the {@action Dash|XPHB} action as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Climber.entries.1.name: Wall Walker
Climber.name: Climber
Cloak of Flies.entries.0: As a bonus action, you can surround yourself with a magical aura that looks like buzzing flies. The aura extends 5 feet from you in every direction, but not through {@quickref Cover||3||total cover}. It lasts until you're {@condition incapacitated} or you dismiss it as a bonus action.
Cloak of Flies.entries.1: The aura grants you advantage on Charisma ({@skill Intimidation}) checks but disadvantage on all other Charisma checks. Any other creature that starts its turn in the aura takes poison damage equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of 0 damage).
Cloak of Flies.entries.2: Once you use this invocation, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.
Cloak of Flies.name: Cloak of Flies
Cloud Rune.entries.0: This rune emulates the deceptive magic used by some cloud giants. While wearing or carrying an object inscribed with this rune, you have advantage on Dexterity ({@skill Sleight of Hand}) checks and Charisma ({@skill Deception}) checks.
Cloud Rune.entries.1: In addition, when you or a creature you can see within 30 feet of you is hit by an attack roll, you can use your reaction to invoke the rune and choose a different creature within 30 feet of you, other than the attacker. The chosen creature becomes the target of the attack, using the same roll. This magic can transfer the attack's effects regardless of the attack's range. Once you invoke this rune, you can't do so again until you finish a short or long rest.
Cloud Rune.name: Cloud Rune
Commanders Strike.entries.0: When you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn, you can replace one of your attacks to direct one of your companions to strike. When you do so, choose a willing creature who can see or hear you and expend one Superiority Die. That creature can immediately use its {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to make one attack with a weapon or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, adding the Superiority Die to the attack's damage roll on a hit.
Commanders Strike.name: Commander's Strike
Commanding Insight.entries.0: Those who attempt to deceive you do so in vain. You have proficiency in the {@skill Insight|XPHB} skill.
Commanding Insight.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Insight checks. You can use this feature a number of times equal to twice your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Commanding Insight.entries.1.name: Exceptional Insight
Commanding Insight.name: Commanding Insight
Commanding Presence.entries.0: When you make a Charisma ({@skill Intimidation|XPHB}, {@skill Performance|XPHB}, or {@skill Persuasion|XPHB}) check, you can expend one Superiority Die and add that die to the roll.
Commanding Presence.name: Commanding Presence
Connection to Nature.entries.0: You've learned that paying attention to the environment around you is the best way to predict its threats. You have proficiency in the {@skill Nature|XPHB} skill.
Connection to Nature.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Nature checks. You can use this feature a number of times equal to twice your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Connection to Nature.entries.1.name: Bond with Nature
Connection to Nature.name: Connection to Nature
Constricting Tendrils.entries.0: You gain an additional option for your Writing Tendrils mutation.
Constricting Tendrils.entries.1: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} while Writhing Tentacles is active, you can attempt to restrain a creature within 5 feet of you with your tendrils. The creature must succeed on a Strength or Dexterity saving throw or gain the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition. The DC is 8 plus your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} plus your Transformation Stage. A {@condition Restrained|XPHB} creature can take an action to make a Strength ({@skill Athletics|XPHB}) check against this DC, ending the condition on itself on a success. The {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition ends if you are {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB}, you move from your current position, if you retract your tendrils, or if you use your tendrils to perform another ability.
Constricting Tendrils.name: Constricting Tendrils
Corrosive Membrane.entries.0: When you manifest your Ooze Form, it lasts for 10 minutes.
Corrosive Membrane.entries.1: While manifesting your Ooze Form, you are covered with a sheen of acidic slime. When a creature hits you with a melee attack, it takes Acid damage equal to {@dice 1d6} plus your Transformation Stage. Nonmagical ammunition is destroyed immediately after hitting you and dealing damage. Any nonmagical weapon takes a cumulative -1 penalty to attack rolls immediately after dealing damage or coming into contact with you. The weapon is destroyed if the penalty reaches -5. The penalty can be removed by casting the {@spell Mending|XPHB} spell on the weapon.
Corrosive Membrane.entries.2: Additionally, you can eat through 2 feet of nonmagical wood or metal in 1 minute simply by touching it.
Corrosive Membrane.name: Corrosive Membrane
Corrupting Magic.entries.0: Whenever you kill any creature with a spell, you may apply an additional effect depending on the damage dealt. This ability cannot be used when you kill a Construct, Undead, or any creature without a soul.
Corrupting Magic.entries.1: Choose one of the following effects when you use this ability:
Corrupting Magic.entries.2.entries.0: As the soul of your enemy leaves their body, you siphon off part of their life force. You regain {@dice 1d6} {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} per Transformation Stage.
Corrupting Magic.entries.2.name: Life Force
Corrupting Magic.entries.3.entries.0: Choose a creature within 10 feet of the killed creature. That creature takes {@damage 1d6} Poison damage per Transformation Stage.
Corrupting Magic.entries.3.name: Poison
Corrupting Magic.entries.4.entries.0: Choose a creature within 10 feet of the killed creature. That creature gains one {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} level. A creature may only have one {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} level from this feature at a time.
Corrupting Magic.entries.4.name: Exhaustion
Corrupting Magic.name: Corrupting Magic
Crafter's Eye.entries.0: The history of Etharis is written in relics, and you read that history better than most. When you make a {@skill History|XPHB} check related to any object (an item, device, building, or material) and you have proficiency in an {@item Artisan's Tools|XPHB} associated with creating that object, you are considered proficient in {@skill History|XPHB} and you add double your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} to the check instead of your normal bonus.
Crafter's Eye.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the {@skill History|XPHB} checks you make with Crafter's Eye. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Crafter's Eye.entries.1.name: Crafter's Cunning
Crafter's Eye.name: Crafter's Eye
Creature Cover.entries.0: By slipping behind enemies or allies alike, you are able to fade from view with ease. You can take the {@action Hide|XPHB} action even when you have {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Half Cover} from a creature, as long as that creature is of a size larger than you.
Creature Cover.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, you can take the {@action Hide|XPHB} action when you have {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Half Cover} from a creature the same size as you.
Creature Cover.entries.1.name: Subtle Cover
Creature Cover.name: Creature Cover
Creature of Earth.entries.1: You have {@sense Tremorsense|XPHB} with a range of 30 feet. You can spend 2 additional Mutation Points to gain a {@variantrule Burrow Speed|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}.
Creature of Earth.name: Creature of Earth
Creature of the Sea.entries.1: You gain a {@variantrule Swim Speed|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}, and you can breathe underwater. For an additional 1 Mutation Point, you gain a {@variantrule Swim Speed|XPHB} equal to twice your speed.
Creature of the Sea.name: Creature of the Sea
Creature of the Sky.entries.1: You gain a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} of 30 feet. For an additional 2 Mutation Points, your {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} increases to 60 feet.
Creature of the Sky.name: Creature of the Sky
Curse of the Befuddled.entries.1: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, choose a creature that you can see within 60 feet of yourself that is cursed by your Evil Eye and spend 2 Jinx Points. The target must make a Wisdom saving throw.
Curse of the Befuddled.entries.2: On a failed save, the creature has the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} condition for 10 minutes or until you or your allies damage it. When the effect ends, the target knows it was {@condition Charmed|XPHB} by you.
Curse of the Befuddled.entries.3: After you use this Misfortune, the creature is no longer cursed by your Evil Eye.
Curse of the Befuddled.name: Curse of the Befuddled
Curse of the Clumsy.entries.1: When a creature cursed by your Evil Eye moves at least 5 feet on its turn, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} and spend 3 Jinx Points to cause the creature to have the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition and have its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} reduced to 0 until the end of its turn.
Curse of the Clumsy.entries.2: After you use this Misfortune, the creature is no longer cursed by your Evil Eye.
Curse of the Clumsy.name: Curse of the Clumsy
Curse of the Debilitated.entries.1: When a creature cursed by your Evil Eye takes damage, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} and spend 1 Jinx Point to roll {@dice 1d12}. The creature takes Necrotic damage equal to the roll.
Curse of the Debilitated.entries.2: After you use this Misfortune, the creature is no longer cursed by your Evil Eye.
Curse of the Debilitated.name: Curse of the Debilitated
Curse of the Doomed.entries.1: When you miss with an attack roll against a creature cursed by your Evil Eye, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} and spend 1 Jinx Point to reroll the attack. You must use the new roll.
Curse of the Doomed.entries.2: After you use this Misfortune, the creature is no longer cursed by your Evil Eye.
Curse of the Doomed.name: Curse of the Doomed
Curse of the Fearful.entries.1: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, choose a creature that you can see within 60 feet of yourself that is cursed by your Evil Eye and spend 2 Jinx Points. The target must make a Wisdom saving throw.
Curse of the Fearful.entries.2: On a failed save, the creature has the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition for 1 minute. At the end of each of its turns, the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} target repeats the save, ending the Misfortune on itself on a success.
Curse of the Fearful.entries.3: After you use this Misfortune, the creature is no longer cursed by your Evil Eye.
Curse of the Fearful.name: Curse of the Fearful
Curse of the Inept.entries.1: After a creature you can see that is cursed by your Evil Eye makes a {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB}, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} and spend 1 Jinx Point to make the target reroll, and it must use the new roll.
Curse of the Inept.entries.2: After you use this Misfortune, the creature is no longer cursed by your Evil Eye.
Curse of the Inept.name: Curse of the Inept
Curse of the Insensate.entries.1: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, choose a creature that you can see that is cursed by your Evil Eye and spend 3 Jinx Points. The target must make a Wisdom saving throw.
Curse of the Insensate.entries.2: On a failed save, the creature has the {@condition Blinded|XPHB} and {@condition Deafened|XPHB} conditions for 1 minute. At the end of each of its turns, the target repeats the save, ending the Misfortune on itself on a success.
Curse of the Insensate.entries.3: After you use this Misfortune, the creature is no longer cursed by your Evil Eye.
Curse of the Insensate.name: Curse of the Insensate
Curse of the Maimed.entries.1: When you hit a creature cursed by your Evil Eye with an attack roll using a weapon or {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} and roll a 19 or 20 on the {@dice d20}, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} and spend 2 Jinx Points to score a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB}.
Curse of the Maimed.entries.2: After you use this Misfortune, the creature is no longer cursed by your Evil Eye.
Curse of the Maimed.name: Curse of the Maimed
Curse of the Marked.entries.1: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can spend 2 Jinx Points to increase the duration of your Evil Eye to 1 hour or until you mark a different creature with your Evil Eye, whichever comes first.
Curse of the Marked.entries.2: Additionally, while a creature is affected by this curse, you always know the direction and distance to the creature while the two of you are on the same plane of existence.
Curse of the Marked.name: Curse of the Marked
Curse of the Plagued.entries.1: When a creature you can see that is cursed by your Evil Eye would regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} and spend 1 Jinx Point to halve the number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} regained. Afterward, the creature can't regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} until the start of your next turn.
Curse of the Plagued.entries.2: After you use this Misfortune, the creature is no longer cursed by your Evil Eye.
Curse of the Plagued.name: Curse of the Plagued
Curse of the Somnolent.entries.1: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, choose a creature you can see that is cursed by your Evil Eye and spend 3 Jinx Points. The target must make a Wisdom saving throw.
Curse of the Somnolent.entries.2: On a failed save, the creature has the {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} condition for 1 minute. The condition ends on a target if it takes damage or someone within 5 feet of it takes an action to shake it out of the Misfortune's effect.
Curse of the Somnolent.entries.3: Creatures that don't sleep, such as elves, or that have {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to the {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} condition automatically succeed on saves against this Misfortune.
Curse of the Somnolent.entries.4: After you use this Misfortune, the creature is no longer cursed by your Evil Eye.
Curse of the Somnolent.name: Curse of the Somnolent
Curse of the Unlucky.entries.1: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can spend 3 Jinx Points to put a pox on a creature cursed by your Evil Eye. Whenever the creature is about to make an attack roll or saving throw, you can roll {@dice 1d4} and subtract the number rolled from the total.
Curse of the Unlucky.entries.2: This Misfortune ends when the creature is no longer cursed by your Evil Eye.
Curse of the Unlucky.name: Curse of the Unlucky
Cursed Claw.entries.0: You gain a Claw attack. This attack uses either your Strength or Dexterity for attack and damage rolls.
Cursed Claw.entries.1: You can use this attack twice as part of an {@action Attack|XPHB} action, or once as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. The Claw attack deals {@dice 2d6} plus your Strength or Dexterity modifier.
Cursed Claw.entries.2: Additionally, when the Claw attack hits, the target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or be afflicted with a random Shadowsteel curse at Stage 1. The saving throw DC is 8 plus your Constitution modifier plus your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}. A creature already afflicted with a Shadowsteel curse cannot gain another Shadowsteel curse with this attack.
Cursed Claw.name: Cursed Claw
Daemonic Brand.entries.0: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} on your turn, you can brand a creature within 60 feet of you that you can see with a fiery mark that remains on them for 1 minute. If the target succeeds on a Wisdom saving throw, they are not branded. The DC of the saving throw is 8 plus your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} plus your Transformation Stage.
Daemonic Brand.entries.1: If the creature fails the saving throw, you choose one of the following effects that lasts for the duration:
Daemonic Brand.entries.2.items.0: The creature takes a -2 penalty on all saving throws.
Daemonic Brand.entries.2.items.1: The first attack against the creature on each turn is made with {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB}.
Daemonic Brand.entries.2.items.2: The creature cannot regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}.
Daemonic Brand.entries.3: You can use this ability a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and you regain all uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Daemonic Brand.name: Daemonic Brand
Damage Resistance (Armor Modification).e: The monster hide used to craft your armor grants some resistances. You gain {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to two of the following damage types of your choice: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, Poison, or Thunder.
Damage Resistance.entries.0: Exposure to the worst effects of a specific energy has given you a tolerance to its effects. You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to one of the following damage types of your choice: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, Poison, or Thunder.
Damage Resistance.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, as a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to taking damage of the type you chose for {@variantrule Damage|XPHB} {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB}, you gain {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to that damage type until the end of your next turn. You regain the use this feature when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}.
Damage Resistance.entries.1.name: Damage Immunity
Damage Resistance.name: Damage Resistance
Darkvision.entries.0: A life spent in shadow has made you grow accustomed to the gloom. You can see in {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} within 60 feet of you as if it were {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB}, and in {@variantrule Darkness|XPHB} within 60 feet of you as if it were {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB}. You can't discern color in {@variantrule Darkness|XPHB}, only shades of gray.
Darkvision.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, the range of your {@sense Darkvision|XPHB} increases to 120 feet.
Darkvision.entries.1.name: Improved Darkvision
Darkvision.name: Darkvision
Debilitating Magic.entries.0: Choose one ability score. You lose 2 points in that ability as the power of the Shadowsteel slowly eats away at your body, soul, or both.
Debilitating Magic.name: Debilitating Magic
Devilish Contractor.entries.0: You have acquired the ability to bind mortal creatures to your will. You can create a contract to bind a mortal Humanoid's soul to you, feeding on its power. The mortal gains a gift asked for in the contract, and you gain a Gift of Damnation of your choice (see Gifts of Damnation).
Devilish Contractor.entries.1: To bind a mortal's soul to you, you must first create a contract for their desired gift. A contract requires magical ink and paper worth 10 GP for each Transformation Stage you have acquired.
Devilish Contractor.entries.2: You and the mortal must both sign the contract willingly, fully aware of the costs involved. Once signed, daemonic entities give you a Gift of Damnation, and the mortal receives their desired wishes within 7 days. You do not have to provide this boon yourself.
Devilish Contractor.entries.3: For example, upon signing a contract for a {@optfeature Gift of Unfettered Glory|GrimHollowPG24}, you receive the benefits listed for that gift. The mortal who signs receives their stated desire, provided by the dubious powers of the Netherworld. You cannot benefit directly from the mortal's desired gift, nor can an ally. No ally of yours can ever sign a contract with you.
Devilish Contractor.entries.4: You may take a Gift of Damnation of the Stage that you have attained or lower. For example, if you are at Stage 2 of the Transformation, you may only take a Stage 1 or Stage 2 Gift of Damnation.
Devilish Contractor.entries.5: There is no limit to the number of contracts you can execute, but you can only have one active Gift of Damnation at a time. When you would gain a second Gift of Damnation, you may replace your active gift with the new one. Also, when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} you may swap your active Gift of Damnation for any other gift associated with a signed contract.
Devilish Contractor.name: Devilish Contractor
Devilish Subcontractor.entries.0: You may gain the benefits of two Gifts of Damnation at the same time. You may still only switch one Gift of Damnation after a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Devilish Subcontractor.name: Devilish Subcontractor
Devils Sight.entries.0: You can see normally in {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} and {@variantrule Darkness|XPHB}—both magical and nonmagical—within 120 feet of yourself.
Devils Sight.name: Devil's Sight
Devouring Blade.entries.0: The Extra Attack of your {@optfeature Thirsting Blade|XPHB} invocation confers two extra attacks rather than one.
Devouring Blade.name: Devouring Blade
Disarming Attack.entries.0: When you hit a creature with an attack roll, you can expend one Superiority Die to attempt to disarm the target. Add the Superiority Die roll to the attack's damage roll. The target must succeed on a Strength saving throw or drop one object of your choice that it's holding, with the object landing in its space.
Disarming Attack.name: Disarming Attack
Disquieting Voice.entries.1: You can comprehend and verbally communicate with Beasts, and you can use any of the {@action Influence|XPHB} action's skill options with them.
Disquieting Voice.name: Disquieting Voice
Distant Spell.entries.0: When you cast a spell that has a range of at least 5 feet, you can spend 1 Sorcery Point to double the spell's range. Or when you cast a spell that has a range of Touch, you can spend 1 Sorcery Point to make the spell's range 30 feet.
Distant Spell.name: Distant Spell
Distracting Strike.entries.0: When you hit a creature with an attack roll, you can expend one Superiority Die to distract the target. Add the Superiority Die roll to the attack's damage roll. The next attack roll against the target by an attacker other than you has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} if the attack is made before the start of your next turn.
Distracting Strike.name: Distracting Strike
Divine Clemency.entries.0: When an ally within 30 feet of you that you can see takes damage, you can use a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to cast {@spell Healing Word|XPHB} at first level on that ally without using a spell slot.
Divine Clemency.entries.1: You can do this a number of times equal to your Transformation Stage. You regain all uses of this feature after finishing a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Divine Clemency.name: Divine Clemency
Divine Sangromancy.entries.0: A connection to the life force of others lets you shape that force to their benefit. Whenever an allied creature within 30 feet of you regains {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, you can spend a {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB|Hit Die} and add the roll of the die to the number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} gained by the ally.
Divine Sangromancy.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, when you use Divine Sangromancy, you also regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB|Hit Die} roll.
Divine Sangromancy.entries.1.name: Sangromancy Savant
Divine Sangromancy.name: Divine Sangromancy
Dodge Roll.entries.0: You can expend one {@classFeature Risk|Gunslinger|ValdaGunslinger|2|ValdaGunslinger|Risk Die} as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to move up to 15 feet and reload any Ranged weapon you are holding. This movement doesn't provoke {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks} and is unaffected by {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB}.
Dodge Roll.name: Dodge Roll
Draining Attack.entries.0: As your enemy's life force ebbs, you grow ever stronger. If you have the {@optfeature Natural Attack|GrimHollowPG24} trait, each time you hit with an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, you gain {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the damage dealt by the attack.
Draining Attack.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, when you use Draining Attack, the target also takes a penalty to their {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} maximum equal to the damage dealt by the attack. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Draining Attack.entries.1.name: To the Dregs
Draining Attack.name: Draining Attack
Draining Flight.entries.0: You can now use your {@optfeature Incorporeal Movement|GrimHollowPG24} feature as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. Additionally, your {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} while using {@optfeature Haunting Flight|GrimHollowPG24} becomes double your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}.
Draining Flight.entries.1: Additionally, while you are using {@optfeature Incorporeal Movement|GrimHollowPG24}, your movement does not provoke {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks} and you can damage creatures that you move through. Once per turn, when you enter a space occupied by a creature, you can choose to force that creature to succeed on a Constitution saving throw or take {@damage 6d6} Psychic damage and have the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition until the end of your next turn. A creature takes half damage on a successful save and is not {@condition Frightened|XPHB}. The DC of the Constitution saving throw is 8 plus your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} plus your Transformation Stage.
Draining Flight.entries.2: Once you use this feature, you must finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} before you can use it again.
Draining Flight.name: Draining Flight
Draught of Bear's Endurance.entries.0: For 10 minutes, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Constitution checks and Constitution saving throws.
Draught of Bear's Endurance.name: Draught of Bear's Endurance
Draught of Bull's Strength.entries.0: For 10 minutes, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Strength checks and Strength saving throws.
Draught of Bull's Strength.name: Draught of Bull's Strength
Draught of Cat's Grace.entries.0: For 10 minutes, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Dexterity checks and Dexterity saving throws.
Draught of Cat's Grace.name: Draught of Cat's Grace
Draught of Eagle's Splendor.entries.0: For 10 minutes, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Charisma checks and Charisma saving throws.
Draught of Eagle's Splendor.name: Draught of Eagle's Splendor
Draught of Fox's Cunning.entries.0: For 10 minutes, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Intelligence checks and Intelligence saving throws.
Draught of Fox's Cunning.name: Draught of Fox's Cunning
Draught of Owl's Wisdom.entries.0: For 10 minutes, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Wisdom checks and Wisdom saving throws.
Draught of Owl's Wisdom.name: Draught of Owl's Wisdom
Drawn to Darkness.entries.0: Your body and soul are bound to the cold darkness of the realms of death. You have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on {@variantrule Death Saving Throw|XPHB|Death Saving Throws} as your life force is drawn to oblivion.
Drawn to Darkness.name: Drawn to Darkness
Dread Venom.entries.0: A foul-smelling blade oil that causes wounds to hemorrhage and refuse healing. Dread Venom can coat one Slashing or Piercing weapon or up to 10 pieces of Slashing or Piercing ammunition. Applying the oil takes 1 minute and the oil lasts 8 hours once applied. When a creature takes damage from the {@condition poisoned} item, it can't regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} until it finishes a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Dread Venom.name: Dread Venom
Dreadful Word.entries.0: You can cast {@spell confusion} once using a warlock spell slot. You can't do so again until you finish a long rest.
Dreadful Word.name: Dreadful Word
Dreams and Nightmares.entries.0: You gain the ability to manipulate the mind of mortal beings. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, choose a Humanoid you can see within 30 feet of you. That creature must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw. The DC is 8 plus your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} plus your Transformation Stage.
Dreams and Nightmares.entries.1: On a failed saving throw, the creature has the {@condition Paralyzed|XPHB} condition, as it is stuck in a blissful dream or a terrifying nightmare. The condition lasts for 1 minute. The target can attempt the Constitution saving throw at the end of each of its turns and each time it takes damage.
Dreams and Nightmares.entries.2: You can choose to concentrate on the effect as you would a spell. If you are {@status Concentration|XPHB|Concentrating} on the effect when the target attempts its saving throw, the target has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the saving throw.
Dreams and Nightmares.entries.3: You can use this ability a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and you regain all uses of this ability when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Dreams and Nightmares.name: Dreams and Nightmares
Dreamwalking.entries.0: Whenever you rest, you touch the dreams of those around you, seeding their thoughts and memories into your own mind. When you make an ability check to recall lore or knowledge, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the check. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Dreamwalking.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, you gain an instinctive knowledge of the secrets of other creatures while you touch their dreams. Using a {@action Search|XPHB} action, you focus on one creature you can see and make a DC 15 Wisdom ({@skill Insight|XPHB}) check. With a successful check, you learn one secret of the GM's choice known to that creature. The secrets of creatures that don't have a language come to you as vague images and impressions. You regain the use of this feature when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Dreamwalking.entries.1.name: Secret Dreams
Dreamwalking.name: Dreamwalking
Driver.entries.0: The roads and waterways of Etharis are often no less dangerous than the open wilderness, and you dedicate yourself to moving others safely on those routes. You have proficiency with {@item Navigator's Tools|XPHB}, and you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on ability checks made to drive a vehicle.
Driver.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, you can make checks involving driving a vehicle that require an action without having to use your action. You can only get this free use once per round.
Driver.entries.1.name: Remarkable Driver
Driver.name: Driver
Druid.entries.0: You can cast the {@spell Polymorph|XPHB} spell without expending a spell slot. When you cast the {@spell Polymorph|XPHB} spell with this talent, you can target yourself only and the maximum {@variantrule Challenge Rating|XPHB} of the Beast you can transform into equals half your Druid level.
Druid.entries.1: Once you cast the spell using this talent, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Druid.name: Druid
Dual Nature.entries.0: You add a second element to your {@optfeature Elemental Affinity|GrimHollowPG24|Elemental Affinity Boon}. When you do so, you gain the associated benefits of your new element.
Dual Nature.name: Dual Nature
Eager Deceiver.entries.0: You long ago learned that being open with others only gives them power over you. You have proficiency in the {@skill Deception|XPHB} skill.
Eager Deceiver.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Deception checks. You can use this feature a number of times equal to twice your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Eager Deceiver.entries.1.name: Expert Deceiver
Eager Deceiver.name: Eager Deceiver
Echolocation.entries.1: You have {@variantrule Blindsight|XPHB} with a range of 30 feet while you don't have the {@condition Deafened|XPHB} condition.
Echolocation.name: Echolocation
Efficient Killer.entries.0: When you make an attack with your eldritch limbs, you can gain an added ability based on the damage type you have chosen.
Efficient Killer.entries.1.entries.0: You may replace any attack made with your eldritch limb with a razor-sharp barb hurled at the target. You are considered proficient with this barb, which has a Range of 20/60, has the {@itemProperty F|XPHB|Finesse} and {@itemProperty T|XPHB|Thrown} properties, and deals {@damage 2d6} Piercing damage on a hit.
