Efficient Killer.entries.1.name: Piercing
Efficient Killer.entries.2.entries.0: You manifest a long tentacle, tipped with a hardened bone club. Your eldritch limb attack deals an additional {@damage 1d8} Bludgeoning damage. Also, the attack uses the {@itemMastery Slow|XPHB} Weapon Property, which you have Mastery with.
Efficient Killer.entries.2.name: Bludgeoning
Efficient Killer.entries.3.entries.0: Your arm forms a row of viciously hooked claws and talons. Your eldritch limb attack deals an additional {@damage 1d8} Slashing damage. Also, the attack uses the {@itemMastery Graze|XPHB} Weapon Property, which you have Mastery with.
Efficient Killer.entries.3.name: Slashing
Efficient Killer.name: Efficient Killer
Elastic Limbs.entries.0: Your limbs have become elastic and tacky, allowing you to reach distant enemies and climb walls.
Elastic Limbs.entries.1: Your reach increases by 5 feet and you gain a {@variantrule Climb Speed|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}. You can move up, down, and across vertical surfaces and along ceilings, while leaving your hands free. Creatures you are grappling have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Strength ({@skill Athletics|XPHB}) and Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics|XPHB}) checks to escape.
Elastic Limbs.name: Elastic Limbs
Eldritch Aberration.entries.0: You gain the ability to deliver jolts of magical energy with your eldritch limbs. Once per turn, when you hit a creature with an attack using your eldritch limbs, you can expend a spell slot to deal additional damage equal to {@dice 1d6} per spell level expended. You can choose for this additional damage to be of any of the following types: Acid, Cold, Fire, Force, Lightning, or Thunder. If any of these extra dice show a 6 when rolled, the target has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition.
Eldritch Aberration.name: Eldritch Aberration
Eldritch Assault.entries.1: You gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls you make with your Wild Shape form's attacks.
Eldritch Assault.entries.2.entries.0: You can gain this Mutation more than once, but no more than three times.
Eldritch Assault.entries.2.name: Repeatable
Eldritch Assault.name: Eldritch Assault
Eldritch Concentration.entries.0: Your supernatural ability with magic manifests in new ways. When you cast a spell that requires {@status Concentration|XPHB}, if you are already {@status Concentration|XPHB|Concentrating} on one such spell, you can spend the charge of your soul vessel. If you do this, you do not lose {@status Concentration|XPHB} on the original spell. Instead, you gain 1 {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} level.
Eldritch Concentration.entries.1: If you cast a third {@status Concentration|XPHB} spell during this time, or lose {@status Concentration|XPHB} for any other reason, you lose {@status Concentration|XPHB} on both current spells you are {@status Concentration|XPHB|Concentrating} on.
Eldritch Concentration.entries.2: Once you use this feature you cannot use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Eldritch Concentration.name: Eldritch Concentration
Eldritch Mind.entries.0: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Constitution saving throws that you make to maintain {@status Concentration|XPHB}.
Eldritch Mind.name: Eldritch Mind
Eldritch Omniscience.entries.0: You have better mastered the magical arts. The number of spells you can learn and can have prepared increases by 4. Additionally, whenever you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} you gain 1 additional spell slot of whichever level you choose.
Eldritch Omniscience.entries.1: If a creature dies as a result of one of your spells, you can instantly draw that soul into your soul vessel without using a {@action Magic|XPHB} action or {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}.
Eldritch Omniscience.name: Eldritch Omniscience
Eldritch Sight.entries.0: You can cast {@spell detect magic} at will, without expending a spell slot.
Eldritch Sight.name: Eldritch Sight
Eldritch Smite.entries.0: Once per turn when you hit a creature with your pact weapon, you can expend a Pact Magic spell slot to deal an extra {@damage 1d8} Force damage to the target, plus another {@dice 1d8} per level of the spell slot, and you can give the target the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition if it is Huge or smaller.
Eldritch Smite.name: Eldritch Smite
Eldritch Spear.entries.0: Choose one of your known {@filter Warlock cantrips|spells|level=0|class=Warlock|damage type=acid;bludgeoning;cold;fire;force;lightning;necrotic;piercing;poison;psychic;radiant;slashing;thunder|range=point} that deals damage and has a range of 10+ feet. When you cast that spell, its range increases by a number of feet equal to 30 times your Warlock level.
Eldritch Spear.entries.1.entries.0.entri: You can gain this invocation more than once. Each time you do so, choose a different eligible cantrip.
Eldritch Spear.entries.1.entries.0.name: Repeatable
Eldritch Spear.name: Eldritch Spear
Elemental Affinity.entries.0: Though you have sway over all the elements, your Primordial change was sparked by one particular element: your Elemental Affinity. This element infuses your form and dictates your powers. You must choose one element below and gain all the benefits of that element:
Elemental Affinity.entries.1.items.0.ent: You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Lightning damage. In addition, you can channel the air currents around you to guide a ranged attack. Once on each of your turns, when you make an attack with a Ranged weapon or ranged spell, you can make that attack with {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB}. If you are in a location without air—such as underwater or in a vacuum—this feature has no effect.
Elemental Affinity.entries.1.items.1.ent: You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Bludgeoning damage. In addition, whenever you gain {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB}, you gain additional {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}.
Elemental Affinity.entries.1.items.2.ent: You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Fire damage. In addition, whenever you deal Fire damage, you can add your Constitution modifier to the damage dealt.
Elemental Affinity.entries.1.items.3.ent: You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Cold damage. In addition, whenever a creature within 30 feet regains {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, you can use a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to imbue them with healing elemental energy. The creature regains an additional {@dice 1d6} {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}.
Elemental Affinity.name: Elemental Affinity
Elemental Ammunition.entries.0: The head of an arrow or bolt is tipped with poison, loaded with an acid vial, dipped in flammable oil, or coated with another substance. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you imbue with elemental power a single piece of ammunition that can be fired from a Longbow, Shortbow, or Crossbow as part of an {@action Attack|XPHB} action. When an Elemental Ammunition is imbued, choose a damage type: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, Poison, or Thunder. When a creature takes damage from the Elemental Ammunition, it takes an additional {@damage 2d6} damage of the chosen type.
Elemental Ammunition.entries.1: Elemental Ammunition discharges its energy if it hits, and it can't be recovered. If the attack misses, the Elemental Ammunition can be recovered and used again. Elemental Ammunition retains its power for 48 hours.
Elemental Ammunition.name: Elemental Ammunition
Elemental Attunement.entries.0: You can use your action to briefly control elemental forces within 30 feet of you, causing one of the following effects of your choice:
Elemental Attunement.entries.1.items.0: Create a harmless, instantaneous sensory effect related to air, earth, fire, or water, such as a shower of sparks, a puff of wind, a spray of light mist, or a gentle rumbling of stone.
Elemental Attunement.entries.1.items.1: Instantaneously light or snuff out a candle, a torch, or a small campfire.
Elemental Attunement.entries.1.items.2: Chill or warm up to 1 pound of nonliving material for up to 1 hour.
Elemental Attunement.entries.1.items.3: Cause earth, fire, water, or mist that can fit within a 1-foot cube to shape itself into a crude form you designate for 1 minute.
Elemental Attunement.name: Elemental Attunement
Elemental Charge (Armor Modification).en: You embed your armor with a Construct's gemstone or infuse it with the power of an Elemental. Choose one of the following damage types: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, Poison, or Thunder. When you hit a creature with an attack, you can cause it to deal the chosen damage type rather than its normal damage type, and the attack deals an extra {@dice 1d6} of that type.
Elemental Imbalance.entries.0: Your body reacts in strange ways to the application of severe elemental damage. When you take Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder damage, roll {@dice 1d6}. On a 1, your Primordial form reacts in a volatile manner, and the following effects occur:
Elemental Imbalance.entries.1.items.0: You gain {@variantrule Vulnerability|XPHB} to the instance of damage you just took, even if you had {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} or {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to that damage.
Elemental Imbalance.entries.1.items.1: Creatures within 5 feet of you take the original amount of damage that you took. A DC 15 Constitution saving throw halves this damage.
Elemental Imbalance.name: Elemental Imbalance
Elemental Inurement.entries.1: You gain {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to one of the following damage types of your choice: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, Poison, or Thunder.
Elemental Inurement.entries.2.entries.0: You can gain this Mutation more than once, but you must choose a different {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} each time.
Elemental Inurement.entries.2.name: Repeatable
Elemental Inurement.name: Elemental Inurement
Elemental Mastery.entries.0: You add the final element to you Elemental Affinity Boon. When you do so, you gain the associated benefits of your new element.
Elemental Mastery.entries.1: Additionally, you can summon the four elements to wreathe you in power. Whenever a creature hits you with a melee attack, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to force it to make a Dexterity saving throw, dealing {@damage 6d6} damage of a type of your choice from Bludgeoning, Cold, Fire, or Lightning, or half as much on a successful save.
Elemental Mastery.entries.2: After you use this {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}, your Elemental Affinity features gain the following benefits until the end of your next turn
Elemental Mastery.entries.3.entries.0: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on all Ranged weapon and ranged spell attacks.
Elemental Mastery.entries.3.name: Air
Elemental Mastery.entries.4.entries.0: At the beginning of your turn, you gain 20 {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB}.
Elemental Mastery.entries.4.name: Earth
Elemental Mastery.entries.5.entries.0: At the beginning of your turn, each creature of your choice within 5 feet of you takes {@damage 2d6} Fire damage.
Elemental Mastery.entries.5.name: Fire
Elemental Mastery.entries.6.entries.0: Whenever a creature withing 60 feet that you can see regains {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} by spending {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB} or through a spell or magical ability, that creature regains an additional 20 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}.
Elemental Mastery.entries.6.name: Water
Elemental Mastery.entries.7: You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Constitution modifier, regaining all uses upon completing a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Elemental Mastery.name: Elemental Mastery
Elemental Surge.entries.0: You can channel pure elemental energy into a concentrated bolt of an element of your choice. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can use any of the following options:
Elemental Surge.entries.1.items.0.entrie: You can make a ranged attack targeting a creature within 60 feet. You are proficient with this attack, which uses your Constitution modifier. On a hit, this attack deals {@dice 3d8} plus your Constitution modifier Lightning damage. You can then use a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to target another creature within 30 feet of the first target with the same attack.
Increase the damage of these attacks by {@dice 1d8} for each Transformation Stage above 2.
Elemental Surge.entries.1.items.0.name: Lightning Strike
Elemental Surge.entries.1.items.1.entrie: You can force a creature within 30 feet to make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes Bludgeoning damage equal to {@dice 3d6} plus your Constitution modifier, or half as much on a successful save. Increase the damage by {@dice 1d6} for each Transformation Stage above 2.
You also gain {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to half the damage dealt.
Elemental Surge.entries.1.items.1.name: Earth Shard
Elemental Surge.entries.1.items.2.entrie: Each creature in a 15-foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone} originating from you makes a Dexterity saving throw against a DC equal to 8 plus your Constitution modifier plus your Transformation Stage. On a failed save, creatures in the area take Fire damage equal to {@dice 2d8} plus your Constitution modifier. Increase the damage by {@dice 1d8} for each Transformation Stage above 2.
Elemental Surge.entries.1.items.2.name: Flame Wave
Elemental Surge.entries.1.items.3.entrie: Choose a creature you can see within 60 feet of you. The creature regains a number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to {@dice 2d8} plus your Constitution modifier. Increase the number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} regained by {@dice 1d8} for each Transformation Stage above 2.
Elemental Surge.entries.1.items.3.name: Aquatic Rejuvenation
Elemental Surge.entries.1.items.4: You can use Elemental Surge a number of times equal to your Constitution modifier, regaining all expended uses upon finishing a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Elemental Surge.name: Elemental Surge
Elfsight Oil.entries.0: For 8 hours, you have {@sense Darkvision|XPHB} with a range of 120 feet.
Elfsight Oil.name: Elfsight Oil
Embrace the Past.entries.0: The lessons of the past are harsh, but learning those lessons might give you the best insight for navigating the future. You have proficiency in the {@skill History|XPHB} skill.
Embrace the Past.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@skill History|XPHB} checks. You can use this feature a number of times equal to twice your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Embrace the Past.entries.1.name: Deep Lore
Embrace the Past.name: Embrace the Past
Empowered Spell.entries.0: When you roll damage for a spell, you can spend 1 Sorcery Point to reroll a number of the damage dice up to your Charisma modifier (minimum of one), and you must use the new rolls.
Empowered Spell.entries.1: You can use Empowered Spell even if you've already used a different Metamagic option during the casting of the spell.
Empowered Spell.name: Empowered Spell
Enemy in Motion.entries.0: A lifetime spent wandering lets you judge when others' movement works to your benefit. When you make an attack roll against a creature or make a saving throw against a creature's attack, spell, or ability, you can use a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the attack roll or saving throw if that creature moved since the end of your last turn. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Enemy in Motion.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, Enemy in Motion also lets you use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to affect an ally's attack roll or saving throw if your ally is within 30 feet.
Enemy in Motion.entries.1.name: Moving Insight
Enemy in Motion.name: Enemy in Motion
Enfeebling Arrow.entries.0: You weave necromantic magic into your arrow. The creature hit by the arrow takes an extra {@damage 2d6} necrotic damage. The target must also succeed on a Constitution saving throw, or the damage dealt by its weapon attacks is halved until the start of your next turn.
Enfeebling Arrow.entries.1: The necrotic damage increases to {@damage 4d6} when you reach 18th level in this class.
Enfeebling Arrow.name: Enfeebling Arrow
Engulf.entries.0: When you manifest your Ooze Form, it lasts for 10 minutes.
Engulf.entries.1: While manifesting your Ooze Form, you can envelop your foes in your gooey body. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can try to engulf a creature of your size category or smaller that is within 5 feet of you. The target makes a Strength saving throw, with a DC equal to 10 plus your Strength modifier plus your Transformation Stage. On a failure, the creature is engulfed.
Engulf.entries.2: While engulfed, a target has {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Total Cover} against attacks and other effects outside of you, and when you move, the engulfed target moves with you. While engulfed, a creature takes {@damage 4d6} Acid damage at the start of each of its turns, is suffocating, has the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition, and repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success. An engulfed creature that is reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} is fully digested, leaving no body or nonmagical equipment behind.
Engulf.name: Engulf
Enhanced Arcane Focus.entries.0: While holding this item, a creature gains a +1 bonus to spell attack rolls. In addition, the creature ignores {@quickref Cover||3||half cover} when making a spell attack.
Enhanced Arcane Focus.entries.1: The bonus increases to +2 when you reach 10th level in this class.
Enhanced Arcane Focus.name: Enhanced Arcane Focus
Enhanced Contract.entries.0: You may switch between your active Gift of Damnation at the end of a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. In addition, when you switch Gifts of Damnation, you gain {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to 5 times your Transformation Stage.
Enhanced Contract.name: Enhanced Contract
Enhanced Defense.entries.0: A creature gains a +1 bonus to Armor Class while wearing (armor) or wielding (shield) the infused item.
Enhanced Defense.entries.1: The bonus increases to +2 when you reach 10th level in this class.
Enhanced Defense.name: Enhanced Defense
Enhanced Weapon.entries.0: This magic weapon grants a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with it.
Enhanced Weapon.entries.1: The bonus increases to +2 when you reach 10th level in this class.
Enhanced Weapon.name: Enhanced Weapon
Enlarge.entries.1: Your size increases by one category (from Medium to Large, for example). You can spend an additional 2 Mutation Points to increase your size by two categories instead (from Medium to Huge, for example), gaining the benefits of the spell again.
Enlarge.entries.2: See the {@spell Enlarge/Reduce|XPHB} spell to determine the benefits gained from this ability.
Enlarge.name: Enlarge
Entropic Abomination.entries.0: This Flaw replacees the Stage 3 Flaw: Unstable Existence. The very essence of magic and the effect of stress aggravates the unstable nature of your aberrant body. Each time you fail a saving throw, or the first time you become {@condition Bloodied|XPHB} after finishing a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you must roll on the {@table Unstable Form|GrimHollowPG24|Unstable Form table}. If the result is less than your current Unstable Form effect, replace it with the new result.
Entropic Abomination.name: Entropic Abomination
Environmental Awareness.entries.0: The natural world is a dangerous place, and your connection to specific parts of that world grants you an edge in survival. Choose an environment: arctic, coastal, desert, forest, grassland, hill and mountain, swamp, subterranean, or underwater. While in that environment, whenever you make an ability check to assess structures, monuments, or natural features; to find food or drinkable water; or to track creatures, you are considered to have proficiency in the appropriate skill for the check, and you add double your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} to the check instead of your normal bonus.
Environmental Awareness.entries.1.entrie: If you take this trait multiple times, you gain its benefit for a new environment each time.

Additionally, when you make an ability check using Environmental Awareness, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the check. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Environmental Awareness.entries.1.name: Adaptive Awareness
Environmental Awareness.name: Environmental Awareness
Eternal Mountain Defense.entries.0: You can spend 5 ki points to cast {@spell stoneskin}, targeting yourself.
Eternal Mountain Defense.name: Eternal Mountain Defense
Ethereal Fade.entries.0: Shifting away from the mortal world lets you move through and observe that world unseen. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you fade from the Material Plane into the Ethereal Plane for 1 minute. While you remain in this state, you can't interact with the Material Plane, and effects on the Material Plane can't affect you, including spells and creatures. You can move and hear as normal, and you see everything in shades of gray. When the effect ends, you reappear in the Material Plane in the closest unoccupied space to where you faded from. You can end the effect early as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. You regain the use of this feature again when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Ethereal Fade.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} when making Wisdom checks as part of a {@action Search|XPHB} action.
Ethereal Fade.entries.1.name: Ethereal Focus
Ethereal Fade.name: Ethereal Fade
Ethereal Phasing.entries.0: You can cast the {@spell Blink|XPHB} spell without expending a spell slot. You can cast the spell using this Boon a number of times equal to your Transformation Stage, and you regain all uses after finishing a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Ethereal Phasing.entries.1: While you are under the effects of Blink when cast in this way, you also gain {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} plus your Transformation Stage at the start of each of your turns.
Ethereal Phasing.name: Ethereal Phasing
Evasive Footwork.entries.0: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend one Superiority Die and take the {@action Disengage|XPHB} action. You also roll the die and add the number rolled to your AC until the start of your next turn.
Evasive Footwork.name: Evasive Footwork
Even in Sleep.entries.0: An instinctive sense for danger protects you at all times. While you have the {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} condition while asleep, you are aware of your surroundings and can make {@skill Perception|XPHB} checks normally.
Even in Sleep.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, while you are asleep, you automatically detect the presence of any creature intending harm to you that moves within 30 feet of you. A creature that is simply capable of harming you does not trigger this trait until it has intent to do so. For example, a wild animal might approach you cautiously, then decide to attack only when it realizes you are sleeping.
Even in Sleep.entries.1.name: Sleeping Ward
Even in Sleep.name: Even in Sleep
Evil Eye.entries.0: Your visage is so horrifying that you can cause creatures to drop dead when your gaze falls upon them. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you gaze upon a {@condition Bloodied|XPHB} Beast or Humanoid within 30 feet that can see your true form. The creature must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw with a DC equal to 14 plus your Charisma modifier. If the target fails the save, it drops to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}. On a successful save, the creature takes {@damage 6d8} Psychic damage. You regain the use of this feature when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Evil Eye.name: Evil Eye
Extended Spell.entries.0: When you cast a spell that has a duration of 1 minute or longer, you can spend 1 Sorcery Point to double its duration to a maximum duration of 24 hours.
Extended Spell.entries.1: If the affected spell requires {@status Concentration|XPHB}, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on any saving throw you make to maintain that {@status Concentration|XPHB}.
Extended Spell.name: Extended Spell
Eyes of the Night.entries.0: You gain {@sense Darkvision|XPHB} out to 60 feet. If you already have {@sense Darkvision|XPHB}, the range increases by 60 feet.
Eyes of the Night.name: Eyes of the Night
Eyes of the Rune Keeper.name: Eyes of the Rune Keeper
Fade Away.entries.0: You have learned to avoid notice at all costs, letting you momentarily obscure yourself from observation. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can take the {@action Hide|XPHB} action to conceal yourself without needing to be {@variantrule Heavily Obscured|XPHB} or behind {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Three-Quarters Cover} or {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Total Cover}. You need not be out of a creature's line of sight to use this ability.
Fade Away.entries.1: You become visible at the start of your next turn unless you have moved into a position that allows you to use the {@action Hide|XPHB} action normally. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Fade Away.entries.2.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on your ability check when you take the {@action Hide|XPHB} action from Fade Away, and you become visible at the end of your next turn instead of the start of your next turn.
Fade Away.entries.2.name: Long Fade
Fade Away.name: Fade Away
Fanged Bite.entries.0: As an {@action Attack|XPHB} action, you can make a Bite attack. You are considered proficient with this attack, and you can use Strength or Dexterity as the ability. This attack deals Piercing damage equal to {@dice 1d6} plus your Strength or Dexterity modifier. This Bite attack is not a weapon or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}.
Fanged Bite.entries.1: If the attack hits a creature that has blood, the target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or take an additional {@damage 1d6} Necrotic damage. You regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the Necrotic damage dealt this way. The DC is 8 plus your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} plus your Transformation Stage.
Fanged Bite.name: Fanged Bite
Fangs of the Fire Snake.entries.0: When you use the {@action Attack} action on your turn, you can spend 1 ki point to cause tendrils of flame to stretch out from your fists and feet. Your reach with your unarmed strikes increases by 10 feet for that action, as well as the rest of the turn. A hit with such an attack deals fire damage instead of bludgeoning damage, and if you spend 1 ki point when the attack hits, it also deals an extra {@damage 1d10} fire damage.
Fangs of the Fire Snake.name: Fangs of the Fire Snake
Far Scribe.entries.0: A new page appears in your Book of Shadows. With your permission, a creature can use its action to write its name on that page, which can contain a number of names equal to your proficiency bonus.
Far Scribe.entries.1: You can cast the {@spell sending} spell, targeting a creature whose name is on the page, without using a spell slot and without using material components. To do so, you must write the message on the page. The target hears the message in their mind, and if the target replies, their message appears on the page, rather than in your mind. The writing disappears after 1 minute.
Far Scribe.entries.2: As an action, you can magically erase a name on the page by touching it.
Far Scribe.name: Far Scribe
Favored in House.entries.0: You can request a favor from your house, which can take one of the following forms:
Favored in House.entries.1.items.0: A member of the house casts for you a different spell of level 3 or lower at half the usual cost.
Favored in House.entries.2: You can request such a favor a number of times equal to your Renown Score. If your Renown Score falls below the total number of favors you have requested, a higher-ranking member of the house might call in a favor from you, sending you on a mission for house or personal business.
Favored in House.entries.3: In addition, your house pays you a stipend that supports your Modest lifestyle (1 GP per day).
Favored in House.name: Favored in House
Feinting Attack.entries.0: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend one Superiority Die to feint, choosing one creature within 5 feet of yourself as your target. You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on your next attack roll against that target this turn. If that attack hits, add the Superiority Die to the attack's damage roll.
Feinting Attack.name: Feinting Attack
Fey Form.entries.0: Your creature type becomes Fey in addition to any other creature type(s) you have.
Fey Form.entries.1: Also, after you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, pick a damage type from the following list: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, Psychic, or Thunder. You gain {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to that damage type until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Fey Form.name: Fey Form
Fiend Bound.entries.0: Your body and soul are bound to the Netherworld. You have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on {@variantrule Death Saving Throw|XPHB|Death Saving Throws} as the plane attempts to pull you into it.
Fiend Bound.name: Fiend Bound
Fiend Form.entries.0: Your appearance has grotesquely transformed. Your skin becomes red and leathery, and vicious horns, teeth and nails erupt through the surface of your skin. You can suppress this form and present the appearance of the humanoid you once were, but this is taxing and requires effort. Moments of stress are likely to reveal your true nature. In the following situations your true form may be revealed:
Fiend Form.entries.1.items.4: Entering the presence of Celestials or other Fiends.
Fiend Form.entries.2: In these events, or times of other extreme emotional or physical stress, a GM can call for a Constitution saving throw with a DC based on your current Transformation Stage. If you fail this save, your Horrific Appearance is revealed.
Fiend Form.entries.3.caption: Fiend Form Save DC
Fiend Form.entries.4: Non-evil creatures will typically be instantly {@variantrule Hostile [Attitude]|XPHB|Hostile} to you, unless the GM decides otherwise.
Fiend Form.name: Fiend Form
Fiendish Soul.entries.0: You become a Fiend in addition to any other creature type(s) you have. You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Charisma ({@skill Deception|XPHB}) checks.
Fiendish Soul.entries.1: Additionally, choose one of the following damage types and gain {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to it: Acid, Cold, or Fire.
Fiendish Soul.name: Fiendish Soul
Fiendish Vigor.entries.0: You can cast {@spell False Life|XPHB} on yourself without expending a spell slot. When you cast the spell with this feature, you don't roll the die for the {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB}; you automatically get the highest number on the die.
Fiendish Vigor.name: Fiendish Vigor
Fighter.entries.0: You gain proficiency with {@filter martial weapons|items|type=martial weapon} and training with {@item Shield|XPHB|Shields}. In addition, you gain a {@filter Fighting Style feat|feats|category=FS} of your choice.
Fighter.name: Fighter
Final Fzeg Bloodline.entries.0: You reach the full manifestation of your Fzeg Bloodline. You have the following features:
Final Fzeg Bloodline.entries.1.items.0: Your Strength score increases by 2 points to a maximum of 20.
Final Fzeg Bloodline.entries.1.items.1: The damage done by your Claw attack becomes {@dice 3d6} plus your Strength modifier Slashing damage, using your Strength modifier and {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} on attack rolls. You can also make three Claw attacks with an {@action Attack|XPHB} action.
Final Fzeg Bloodline.entries.1.items.2: You are {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB|Resistant} to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage. Magic and silvered weapons ignore this {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB}.
Final Fzeg Bloodline.name: Final Fzeg Bloodline
Final Soman Bloodline.entries.0: You reach the full manifestation of your Soman Bloodline. You gain the following features:
Final Soman Bloodline.entries.1.items.0: Your Strength and Dexterity scores increase by 1 to a maximum of 20.
Final Soman Bloodline.entries.1.items.1: {@optfeature Fanged Bite|GrimHollowPG24|Fanged Bite's} Necrotic damage increases to {@dice 3d8}.
Final Soman Bloodline.entries.1.items.2: When a Humanoid creature is reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} by your {@optfeature Fanged Bite|GrimHollowPG24} attack or through your feeding, it rises as a {@creature Vampire Spawn|XMM} 24 hours later unless the body is destroyed or undergoes rituals to prevent its rising. You can have two {@creature Vampire Spawn|XMM|Vampire Spawns} created this way under your control, and they follow your verbal commands to the best of their abilities. If you take control of a third {@creature Vampire Spawn|XMM}, you choose one of the other {@creature Vampire Spawn|XMM} you currently control to no longer follow your commands.
Final Soman Bloodline.name: Final Soman Bloodline
Final Strigoi Bloodline.entries.0: You reach the full manifestation of your Strigoi Bloodline. You have the following features:
Your Dexterity score increases by 2 points to a maximum of 20.

You can summon {@dice 2d4} {@creature Swarms of Rats|XMM}, {@creature Swarms of Bats|XMM}, or {@creature Wolf|XMM|Wolves} as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. They appear at the start of your next turn within 60 feet of you. They follow your verbal or mental commands. They disperse after 1 hour, when you are reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, or when you dismiss them with a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. You regain this ability after finishing a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

You can cast the {@spell Misty Step|XPHB} spell up to four times without using a spell slot. You can also cast a spell in this way as a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} when you are targeted with a weapon attack. You cast the spell before taking damage. You regain this ability after finishing a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Final Strigoi Bloodline.name: Final Strigoi Bloodline
Fire Rune.entries.0: This rune's magic channels the masterful craftsmanship of great smiths. While wearing or carrying an object inscribed with this rune, your proficiency bonus is doubled for any ability check you make that uses your proficiency with a tool.
Fire Rune.entries.1: In addition, when you hit a creature with an attack using a weapon, you can invoke the rune to summon fiery shackles: the target takes an extra {@damage 2d6} fire damage, and it must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be {@condition restrained} for 1 minute. While {@condition restrained} by the shackles, the target takes {@damage 2d6} fire damage at the start of each of its turns. The target can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, banishing the shackles on a success. Once you invoke this rune, you can't do so again until you finish a short or long rest.
Fire Rune.name: Fire Rune
Firm Influence.entries.0: Others have learned to fear you—and for good reason. You have proficiency in the {@skill Intimidation|XPHB} skill.
Firm Influence.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Intimidation checks. You can use this feature a number of times equal to twice your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Firm Influence.entries.1.name: Terrifying Influence
Firm Influence.name: Firm Influence
First Strike.entries.0: Hesitation in others is a weakness you've learned to take deadly advantage of. When you hit a creature that hasn't taken a turn in the combat yet, your attack deals an extra {@damage 2d6} damage. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
First Strike.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, you can use the maximum value of the extra damage dice from First Strike, rather than rolling. You regain the use of this feature when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
First Strike.entries.1.name: Strong Strike
First Strike.name: First Strike
Fist of Four Thunders.entries.0: You can spend 2 ki points to cast {@spell thunderwave}.
Fist of Four Thunders.name: Fist of Four Thunders
Flames of the Phoenix.entries.0: You can spend 4 ki points to cast {@spell fireball}.
Flames of the Phoenix.name: Flames of the Phoenix
Fleet of Foot.entries.0: As you've learned more than once, moving fast is often the best way to avoid trouble. Your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases by 5 feet.
Fleet of Foot.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases by another 5 feet, for a total increase of 10 feet.
Fleet of Foot.entries.1.entries.1: Additionally, as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, choose any number of creatures within 30 feet. Those creatures gain a 10 foot bonus to their {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} for 1 minute. You regain the use of this feature when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Fleet of Foot.entries.1.name: Shared Fleetness
Fleet of Foot.name: Fleet of Foot
Fleshknit Phosphate.entries.0: For 1 minute, you regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} at the start of each of your turns if you have at least 1 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point}.
Fleshknit Phosphate.name: Fleshknit Phosphate
Flight (Celestial, Dragon, Elemental).en: For 10 minutes, you grow feathery, feathery, or spectral wings from your back. You gain a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}. You can't benefit from this mutation while wearing Heavy Armor.
Focused Mind.entries.0: Your strength of will protects you from magic that would corrupt your mind. You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against being {@condition Charmed|XPHB}.
Focused Mind.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, when you fail a saving throw against being {@condition Charmed|XPHB}, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to succeed on the save instead. You regain the use of this feature when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Focused Mind.entries.1.name: Immutable Mind
Focused Mind.name: Focused Mind
Focused Reserves.entries.0: No matter how badly beaten down you are, you find the will to keep fighting when you most need it. As a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} after you take damage, you can roll a number of d6s equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} and gain {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the total. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Focused Reserves.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, you can reroll 1s and 2s when you use Focused Reserves, but you must use the new rolls.
Focused Reserves.entries.1.name: Focused Edge
Focused Reserves.name: Focused Reserves
Frayed Thoughts.entries.0: You suffer the effects of sharing a mind with two personalities. Memories of less practical significance are lost to new ones of midnight hunts. You have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Intelligence ability checks and saving throws.
Frayed Thoughts.name: Frayed Thoughts
Fraying Reality.entries.0: Your mind begins to slip into the dark places where the world of life and world of death intersect. When you become {@condition Bloodied|XPHB} for the first time after finishing a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you must make a DC 15 Charisma saving throw. On a failed save, you act as though under the effects of a {@spell Confusion|XPHB} spell for 1 minute.
Fraying Reality.name: Fraying Reality
Friendless.entries.0: You are no longer able to connect with other creatures on a social level. You prefer isolation and twisted contemplation to friendship and camaraderie.
Friendless.entries.1: You cannot be considered an ally to any Humanoids or Beasts, and no living creature can be {@variantrule Friendly [Attitude]|XPHB|Friendly} toward you.
Friendless.entries.2: You also have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on all Charisma ability checks.
Friendless.name: Friendless
Frost Rune.entries.0: This rune's magic evokes the might of those who survive in the wintry wilderness, such as frost giants. While wearing or carrying an object inscribed with this rune, you have advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Animal Handling}) checks and Charisma ({@skill Intimidation}) checks.
Frost Rune.entries.1: In addition, you can invoke the rune as a bonus action to increase your sturdiness. For 10 minutes, you gain a +2 bonus to all ability checks and saving throws that use Strength or Constitution. Once you invoke this rune, you can't do so again until you finish a short or long rest.
Frost Rune.name: Frost Rune
Fzeg Bloodline.entries.0: Your vampirism follows the Fzeg bloodline, a line of vampires that combines the affliction with lycanthropy. You gain the following features:
Fzeg Bloodline.entries.1.items.0: Your Strength score increases by 2. Your Strength score cannot exceed 20 with this feature.
Fzeg Bloodline.entries.1.items.1: You gain a Claw attack. This attack deals {@dice 1d8} plus your Strength modifier Slashing damage, using your Strength modifier and {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} on attack rolls. This Claw attack is not a weapon or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}.
Fzeg Bloodline.entries.1.items.2: When you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action to make the Claw attack, you may use a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to make a Bite attack from {@optfeature Fanged Bite|GrimHollowPG24}.
Fzeg Bloodline.name: Fzeg Bloodline
Gaze of Two Minds.entries.0: You can use a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to touch a willing creature and perceive through its senses until the end of your next turn. As long as the creature is on the same plane of existence as you, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} on subsequent turns to maintain this connection, extending the duration until the end of your next turn. The connection ends if you don't maintain it in this way.
Gaze of Two Minds.entries.1: While perceiving through the other creature's senses, you benefit from any special senses possessed by that creature, and you can cast spells as if you were in your space or the other creature's space if the two of you are within 60 feet of each other.
Gaze of Two Minds.name: Gaze of Two Minds
Ghastly Touch.entries.0: You can deliver a soul-chilling jolt through your attacks. Once per turn, when you hit a target with a melee attack when you are within 5 feet of the target, you can deal an additional {@damage 1d6} Necrotic damage.
Ghastly Touch.entries.1: You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} plus your Transformation Stage. You regain all uses of this feature when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Ghastly Touch.name: Ghastly Touch
Ghostly Gaze.entries.0: As an action, you gain the ability to see through solid objects to a range of 30 feet. Within that range, you have {@sense darkvision} if you don't already have it. This special sight lasts for 1 minute or until your {@status concentration} ends (as if you were {@status concentration||concentrating} on a spell). During that time, you perceive objects as ghostly, transparent images.
Ghostly Gaze.entries.1: Once you use this invocation, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.
Ghostly Gaze.name: Ghostly Gaze
Gift of Joyous Life.entries.0: At the beginning of your turn, if you are {@condition Bloodied|XPHB}, you can choose to roll a {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB|Hit Point Die} (no action required) and regain a number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the roll. If you roll a 1 on this die, you regain no {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} and take 1 point of Force damage instead. You regain this ability when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Gift of Joyous Life.name: Gift of Joyous Life
Gift of Liberating Freedom.entries.0: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} while aren't wearing Heavy armor, you can manifest a pair of leathery wings. You gain a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} equal to half your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}. These wings recede if you dismiss them as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} on your turn, if you have the {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} condition, or if you become {@condition Bloodied|XPHB} while they are manifested.
Gift of Liberating Freedom.entries.1: You can have your wing manifested for 1 hour total, including several shorter stints adding up to 1 hour. You regain your ability to use your wings for an hour after finishing a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Gift of Liberating Freedom.name: Gift of Liberating Freedom
Gift of Martial Prowess.entries.0: Once per turn, when you miss with a weapon attack or {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, you can reroll the attack roll. If the reroll hits, you must spend 3 {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB} and add the result to the attack's normal damage as Force damage. If the reroll misses, you must spend 3 {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB} and take Psychic damage equal to the result. If you have fewer than 3 {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB} available, you cannot use this gift.
Gift of Martial Prowess.entries.1: Once you hit a target with an attack using a rerolled attack, you cannot use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Gift of Martial Prowess.name: Gift of Martial Prowess
Gift of Prodigious Talent.entries.0: Choose two Skills. Gain {@variantrule Expertise|XPHB} in the chosen skills.
Gift of Prodigious Talent.entries.1: If you roll a 1 or 2 on an {@variantrule Ability Check|XPHB} using one of the chosen skills, you immediately lose half your maximum number of {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB}, rounded down. You do not regain {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB} lost this way until you finish two {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB|Long Rests}.
Gift of Prodigious Talent.name: Gift of Prodigious Talent
Gift of Second Chances.entries.0: When you are reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to roll a {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB|Hit Point Die}. If you roll a 2 or better, set your {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} total to the result of the roll. If not, you suffer a {@variantrule Death Saving Throw|XPHB} failure. You regain the use of this gift when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Gift of Second Chances.name: Gift of Second Chances
Gift of Unbridled Power.entries.0: Upon finishing a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}, you can spend 2 {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB} and regain a number of spell slot levels equal to the highest number rolled. However, you take Psychic damage equal to twice the total that you rolled. You regain the reuse of this gift when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Gift of Unbridled Power.name: Gift of Unbridled Power
Gift of Unconditional Love.entries.0: When a creature you can see within 30 feet of you fails a saving throw, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to gain {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to {@dice 1d10} plus your Transformation Stage. You regain the use of this gift when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Gift of Unconditional Love.entries.1: If you roll a 1 on the die, you gain no {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} and instead have the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition.
Gift of Unconditional Love.name: Gift of Unconditional Love
Gift of Unfettered Glory.entries.0: Each time you damage a creature with a melee attack, an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, or a cantrip, you can do an additional 2 points Force Damage per Transformation Stage.
Gift of Unfettered Glory.entries.1: Each time you roll a {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB|Hit Point Die} to regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} during a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}, you regain 2 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} fewer per Hit Die.
Gift of Unfettered Glory.name: Gift of Unfettered Glory
Gift of Unsurpassed Fortune.entries.0: When an enemy you can see within 10 feet succeeds on an attack roll or a saving throw, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to roll {@dice d20}. On a 6 or higher, the triggering attack or save misses or fails, and you regain the use of your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}. Otherwise, you take {@damage 1d6} Psychic damage per Transformation Stage, which cannot be reduced. You regain your use of this ability when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Gift of Unsurpassed Fortune.name: Gift of Unsurpassed Fortune
Gift of the Depths.entries.0: You can breathe underwater, and you gain a {@variantrule Swim Speed|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}.
Gift of the Depths.entries.1: You can also cast {@spell Water Breathing|XPHB} once without expending a spell slot. You regain the ability to cast it in this way again when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Gift of the Depths.name: Gift of the Depths
Gift of the Ever-Living Ones.entries.0: Whenever you regain hit points while your familiar is within 100 feet of you, treat any dice rolled to determine the hit points you regain as having rolled their maximum value for you.
Gift of the Ever-Living Ones.name: Gift of the Ever-Living Ones
Gift of the Protectors.entries.0: A new page appears in your Book of Shadows when you conjure it. With your permission, a creature can take an action to write its name on that page, which can contain a number of names equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of one name).
Gift of the Protectors.entries.1: When any creature whose name is on the page is reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} but not killed outright, the creature magically drops to 1 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} instead. Once this magic is triggered, no creature can benefit from it until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Gift of the Protectors.entries.2: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can erase a name on the page by touching it.
Gift of the Protectors.name: Gift of the Protectors
Gifted Performer.entries.0: When you desire to stand out, you have a natural gift for impressing others. You have proficiency in the {@skill Performance|XPHB} skill.
Gifted Performer.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Performance checks. You can use this feature a number of times equal to twice your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Gifted Performer.entries.1.name: A Sight to Behold
Gifted Performer.name: Gifted Performer
Goading Attack.entries.0: When you hit a creature with an attack roll, you can expend one Superiority Die to attempt to goad the target into attacking you. Add the Superiority Die to the attack's damage roll. The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on attack rolls against targets other than you until the end of your next turn.
Goading Attack.name: Goading Attack
Gong of the Summit.entries.0: You can spend 3 ki points to cast {@spell shatter}.
Gong of the Summit.name: Gong of the Summit
Grandmother's Curse.entries.0: You learn one Shadowsteel curse of your choice. You always have it prepared, and it doesn't count against the number of spells you can prepare each day. You can cast it once per day without using a spell slot or needing spell components.
Grandmother's Curse.name: Grandmother's Curse
Grappling Strike.entries.0: Immediately after you hit a creature with a melee attack on your turn, you can expend one superiority die and then try to grapple the target as a bonus action (see the Player's Handbook for rules on grappling). Add the superiority die to your Strength ({@skill Athletics}) check.
Grappling Strike.name: Grappling Strike
Grasp of Hadar.entries.0: Once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with your {@spell eldritch blast}, you can move that creature in a straight line 10 feet closer to you.
Grasp of Hadar.name: Grasp of Hadar
Grasping Arrow.entries.0: When this arrow strikes its target, conjuration magic creates grasping, poisonous brambles, which wrap around the target. The creature hit by the arrow takes an extra {@damage 2d6} poison damage, its speed is reduced by 10 feet, and it takes {@damage 2d6} slashing damage the first time on each turn it moves 1 foot or more without teleporting. The target or any creature that can reach it can use its action to remove the brambles with a successful Strength ({@skill Athletics}) check against your Arcane Shot save DC. Otherwise, the brambles last for 1 minute or until you use this option again.
Grasping Arrow.entries.1: The poison damage and slashing damage both increase to {@damage 4d6} when you reach 18th level in this class.
Grasping Arrow.name: Grasping Arrow
Grave-Touched Soul.entries.0: You gain {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Necrotic damage. If you already have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Necrotic damage, you gain {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} instead.
Grave-Touched Soul.name: Grave-Touched Soul
Grazing Shot.entries.0: When you miss with a ranged attack roll using a weapon, you can expend one {@classFeature Risk|Gunslinger|ValdaGunslinger|2|ValdaGunslinger|Risk Die} (no action required) to deal damage to that creature equal to a roll of the die plus your Dexterity modifier (minimum of 1). This damage is the same type dealt by the weapon, and the damage can be increased only by increasing the ability modifier. You can only use this maneuver once per turn.
Grazing Shot.name: Grazing Shot
Greater Magic Tricks.entries.0: Your Fey masters provide even further magical abilities. You gain the ability to cast one level 3 spell, one level 4 spell, and one level 5 spell. You can cast each of these once without using a spell slot or needing spell components. You regain the ability to cast these spells when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. The DC for these spells is 8 plus your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} plus your Transformation Stage.
Greater Magic Tricks.entries.1.entries.0: Your spells are {@spell Stinking Cloud|XPHB}, {@spell Vitriolic Sphere|XPHB}, and {@spell Cloudkill|XPHB}.
Greater Magic Tricks.entries.1.name: Spring
Greater Magic Tricks.entries.2.entries.0: Your spells are {@spell Fireball|XPHB}, {@spell Fire Shield|XPHB}, and {@spell Dream|XPHB}.
Greater Magic Tricks.entries.2.name: Summer
Greater Magic Tricks.entries.3.entries.0: Your spells are {@spell Lightning Bolt|XPHB}, {@spell Blight|XPHB}, and {@spell Hold Monster|XPHB}.
Greater Magic Tricks.entries.3.name: Autumn
Greater Magic Tricks.entries.4.entries.0: Your spells are {@spell Vampiric Touch|XPHB}, {@spell Ice Storm|XPHB}, and {@spell Cone of Cold|XPHB}.
Greater Magic Tricks.entries.4.name: Winter
Greater Magic Tricks.name: Greater Magic Tricks
Greater Sanguine Curse.entries.0: Your curse has taken a stronger hold onto you. You gain the following features:
Greater Sanguine Curse.entries.1.items.0: You have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on all {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB|D20 Tests} when you are in sunlight.
Greater Sanguine Curse.name: Greater Sanguine Curse
Hag Form.entries.0: Your {@variantrule Creature Type|XPHB} becomes Fey in addition to any other creature type(s) you have.
Hag Form.entries.1: Additionally, your connection with the magic of the natural world provides you with increased defenses. When you are not wearing armor, your {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB} is 13 plus your Dexterity modifier. Additionally, choose Strength, Intelligence, or Charisma: you are proficient with Saving Throws using that ability score.
Hag Form.name: Hag Form
Hardened Defense (Armor Modification).en: Hardened scales or magical pelts make your armor difficult to pierce. While wearing your crafted armor, you gain a +2 bonus to {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB}.
Haunting Flight.entries.0: You gain a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}.
Haunting Flight.entries.1: Also, while you are flying, you can use a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to focus your terrifying presence on one creature you can see within 30 feet. That creature must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or have the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition for 1 minute. The DC for the Wisdom saving throw is 8 plus your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} plus your Transformation Stage. Once you use this feature, you must finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} before you can use it again.
Haunting Flight.name: Haunting Flight
Healing Resistance.entries.0: You heal at a slower rate than normal, whether that healing is magical or natural. Whenever you regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, you regain half as many {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} as you should. You still recover all {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} at the end of a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Healing Resistance.name: Healing Resistance
Heightened Spell.entries.0: When you cast a spell that forces a creature to make a saving throw, you can spend 2 Sorcery Points to give one target of the spell {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on saves against the spell.
Heightened Spell.name: Heightened Spell
Helping Hand.entries.0: You excel at aiding your allies, knowing that the time will come when you need them to return the favor. You can use the {@action Help|XPHB} action as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to assist any ally making an ability check. (This is an Exploration trait.)
Helping Hand.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, when you use Helping Hand, you can also assist an ally making an attack roll. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. (This is a Combat trait.)
Helping Hand.entries.1.name: Helpful Tactics
Helping Hand.name: Helping Hand
Hideous Appearance (Aberrant Horror).ent: Your appearance has grotesquely transformed. You may have turned into a hulking mass of flesh adorned with countless eyes, or a bone-and-tusk-covered behemoth. Regardless of your true form, you're horrific to behold.

You can suspend this form and take on the appearance of the humanoid you once were, but this is taxing and requires effort. This form is not permanent, and moments of stress are likely to reveal your true nature. Your true form may be revealed in the following situations:

In these events, or times of other extreme emotional or physical stress, a GM can call or a Constitution saving throw with a DC based on your current Transformation Stage. If you fail this save, your Horrific Appearance is revealed.
Non-evil creatures that witness your true form become instantly {@variantrule Hostile [Attitude]|XPHB|Hostile} to you, unless the GM decides otherwise.
Hideous Appearance (Hag).entries.0: Your appearance is grotesquely transformed. Your body, hair, and eyes change in ways mortals find abhorrent. Most creatures witnessing your true form become {@variantrule Hostile [Attitude]|XPHB|Hostile} and no one is likely to trust you, unless the GM decides otherwise. You have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Charisma ({@skill Persuasion|XPHB}) checks.
Hideous Appearance (Hag).entries.1: You cannot stand to look upon your own reflection. The first time you see your reflection after a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you must succeed on a DC 18 Wisdom saving throw or gain 1 {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} level.
Hideous Appearance (Hag).entries.2: Your appearance changes based on your Stage 1 Sisterhood Boon:
Hideous Appearance (Hag).entries.3.entri: You wither or bloat as your body struggles to deal with the ravages of age. Your hair becomes white and several feet in length. Your skin becomes green and covered in warts and boils.
Hideous Appearance (Hag).entries.3.name: Green Sisterhood
Hideous Appearance (Hag).entries.4.entri: Your eyes have turned entirely red, and your pupils are narrow like a cat's. Your skin becomes blood red.
Hideous Appearance (Hag).entries.4.name: Red Sisterhood
Hideous Appearance (Hag).entries.5.entri: You have slimy scales and the pallid skin of a dead fish that sags from your emaciated body. Your hair resembles seaweed, and your eyes are glassy.
Hideous Appearance (Hag).entries.5.name: Sea Sisterhood
Hideous Appearance (Hag).name: Hideous Appearance (Hag)
Hideous Appearance (Lich).entries.0: Your appearance has grotesquely transformed. Your flesh withers and contracts around your bones, your eyes become sunken, and you reek of death. Regardless of your true form, you're horrific to behold.
Hideous Appearance (Lich).entries.1: You can suspend this form and manifest the appearance of the humanoid you once were, but this is taxing and requires effort. This form is not permanent, and moments of stress are likely to reveal your true nature. Your true form may be revealed in the following situations:
Hideous Appearance (Lich).entries.3: In these events, or times of other extreme emotional or physical stress, a GM can call or a Constitution saving throw with a DC based on your current Transformation Stage. If you fail this save, your Horrific Appearance is revealed.
Hideous Appearance (Lich).entries.5: Non-evil creatures that witness your true form become instantly {@variantrule Hostile [Attitude]|XPHB|Hostile} to you, unless the GM decides otherwise.
Hideous Appearance (Lich).name: Hideous Appearance (Lich)
Hill Rune.entries.0: This rune's magic bestows a resilience reminiscent of a hill giant. While wearing or carrying an object that bears this rune, you have advantage on saving throws against being {@condition poisoned}, and you have resistance against poison damage.
Hill Rune.entries.1: In addition, you can invoke the rune as a bonus action, gaining resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage for 1 minute. Once you invoke this rune, you can't do so again until you finish a short or long rest.
Hill Rune.name: Hill Rune
Hold Breath.entries.0: Whether trapped under black water or resisting poisonous fumes, you refuse to give in. You can hold your breath for up to 1 hour.
Hold Breath.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, you can hold your breath for up to 8 hours.
Hold Breath.entries.1.name: Endless Breath
Hold Breath.name: Hold Breath
Holy Strikes.entries.0: When you hit with a weapon or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} or damage a creature with a cantrip, you can add {@damage 1d6} Radiant damage to that attack. You can add this damage a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} plus your Transformation Stage. You regain all uses of this feature when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Holy Strikes.entries.1: At higher Transformation Stages, you deal an additional {@damage 1d6} Radiant damage per Transformation Stage, for a total of {@dice 2d6} at Stage 2, {@dice 3d6} at Stage 3, and {@dice 4d6} at Stage 4.
Holy Strikes.name: Holy Strikes
Homunculus Servant.entries.0: You learn intricate methods for magically creating a special {@creature Homunculus Servant|TCE|homunculus} that serves you. The item you infuse serves as the creature's heart, around which the creature's body instantly forms.
Homunculus Servant.entries.1: You determine the homunculus's appearance. Some artificers prefer mechanical-looking birds, whereas some like winged vials or miniature, animate cauldrons.
Homunculus Servant.entries.2: The homunculus is friendly to you and your companions, and it obeys your commands. See this creature's game statistics in the Homunculus Servant stat block, which uses your proficiency bonus (PB) in several places.
Homunculus Servant.entries.3: In combat, the homunculus shares your initiative count, but it takes its turn immediately after yours. It can move and use its reaction on its own, but the only action it takes on its turn is the {@action Dodge} action, unless you take a bonus action on your turn to command it to take another action. That action can be one in its stat block or some other action. If you are {@condition incapacitated}, the homunculus can take any action of its choice, not just {@action Dodge}.
Homunculus Servant.entries.4: The homunculus regains {@dice 2d6} hit points if the {@spell mending} spell is cast on it. If you or the homunculus dies, it vanishes, leaving its heart in its space.
Homunculus Servant.name: Homunculus Servant
Hunter's Focus.entries.0: While in hybrid form, you can use a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to mark one creature within 60 feet as your prey. A creature remains marked this way for 1 hour, or until it dies. You may only have 1 creature marked at a time.
Hunter's Focus.entries.1: While a creature is marked as your prey, you gain the following benefits:
Hunter's Focus.entries.2.items.0: Whenever you hit the marked creature with a melee attack, you deal an additional {@damage 1d6} damage of that attack's type.
Hunter's Focus.entries.2.items.1: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on any Wisdom ({@skill Perception|XPHB} or {@skill Survival|XPHB}) check you make to find it.
Hunter's Focus.name: Hunter's Focus
Hunter's Instinct.entries.0: You summon a surge of ferocity when your prey least expects it. At the end of each {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you gain a number of d8s equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}. When you make an attack with a weapon or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, you can roll a {@dice d8} and add it to either the attack roll or the damage roll. If you add it to the {@dice d20} roll, you can decide to roll the {@dice d8} after the {@dice d20} roll is made, but you must do so before the outcome of the roll is known.
Hunter's Instinct.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, whenever you use Hunter's Instinct for an attack roll, if the attack roll misses, you retain the {@dice d8} and can use it again.
Hunter's Instinct.entries.1.name: Relentless Instinct
Hunter's Instinct.name: Hunter's Instinct
Hybrid Bear Form.entries.0: You become a Monstrosity in addition to any other creature type(s).
Hybrid Bear Form.entries.1: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you may voluntarily transform into a hybrid half-bear creature. While in your hybrid bear form, the following rules apply:
Hybrid Bear Form.entries.2.items.0: Your Strength score becomes 20 unless it was higher.
Hybrid Bear Form.entries.2.items.1: You gain a {@variantrule Climb Speed|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}.
Hybrid Bear Form.entries.2.items.2: You can't cast spells or concentrate on spells. Your ability to speak is reduced to short, basic, guttural responses.
Hybrid Bear Form.entries.2.items.3: While transformed, you can use weapons and equipment as normal, unless specified elsewhere.
Hybrid Bear Form.entries.2.items.4: You gain a Claw attack that you may use in place of any weapon attack. This attack deals {@dice 1d8} plus your Strength or Dexterity modifier Slashing damage. You are proficient with this attack, and you add either your Strength or Dexterity modifier to attack and damage rolls. This attack is not an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, nor can it be used in place of an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}.
Hybrid Bear Form.entries.2.items.5: Immediately after hitting a target with a Claw attack, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to grab the creature with that hand, as long as it is no more than one size category larger than you. The grabbed target has the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition. The escape DC is 8 plus your Strength modifier plus your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}.
Hybrid Bear Form.entries.3: Your hybrid bear form lasts 1 hour per Transformation Stage. If you entered your hybrid form voluntarily, you can revert to your normal form by using a {@action Magic|XPHB} action on your turn. ({@filter Flaws listed below|optionalfeatures|source=grimhollowpg24|feature type=ly:tf|miscellaneous=} may override this rule.)
Hybrid Bear Form.name: Hybrid Bear Form
Hybrid Form Affinity.entries.0: You have achieved a state of equilibrium that most Lycanthropes never find. You gain the following benefits while you are voluntarily in your hybrid form:
Hybrid Form Affinity.entries.1.items.1: You can cast spells without needing to provide their Verbal or Somatic components. Additionally, you can cast spells without needing to provide material components, provided they do not have a GP value listed.
Hybrid Form Affinity.entries.1.items.2: Allies within 20 feet of you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Wisdom ability checks and saving throws.
Hybrid Form Affinity.name: Hybrid Form Affinity
Hybrid Rat Form.entries.0: You become a Monstrosity in addition to any other creature type(s).
Hybrid Rat Form.entries.1: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you may voluntarily transform into a hybrid half-rat creature. While in your hybrid rat form, the following rules apply:
Hybrid Rat Form.entries.2.items.0: Your Dexterity score becomes 18 unless it was higher.
Hybrid Rat Form.entries.2.items.1: You can {@action Hide|XPHB} or {@action Disengage|XPHB} as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}.
Hybrid Rat Form.entries.2.items.2: You gain proficiency with {@skill Stealth|XPHB}, and you can double your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} when making a Dexterity ({@skill Stealth|XPHB}) check.
Hybrid Rat Form.entries.2.items.3: You can't cast spells or concentrate on spells. Your ability to speak is reduced to short, basic, guttural responses.
Hybrid Rat Form.entries.2.items.4: While transformed, you can use weapons and equipment as normal, unless specified elsewhere.
Hybrid Rat Form.entries.2.items.5: You gain a Claw or Bite attack that you may use in place of any weapon attack. This attack deals {@dice 1d6} plus your Strength or Dexterity modifier Slashing damage. You are proficient with this attack, and you add either your Strength or Dexterity modifier to attack and damage rolls. This attack is not an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, nor can it be used in place of an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}.
Hybrid Rat Form.entries.2.items.6: After taking an {@action Attack|XPHB} action, you may take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} later in that turn to attack once with either your Claw or Bite.
Hybrid Rat Form.entries.2.items.7: If you target a creature that is within 5 feet of one of its enemies, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Claw or Bite attacks made against the target.
Hybrid Rat Form.entries.3: Your hybrid rat form lasts 1 hour per Transformation Stage. If you entered your hybrid form voluntarily, you can revert to your normal form by using a {@action Magic|XPHB} action on your turn. ({@filter Flaws listed below|optionalfeatures|source=grimhollowpg24|feature type=ly:tf|miscellaneous=} may override this rule.)
Hybrid Rat Form.name: Hybrid Rat Form
Hybrid Wolf Form.entries.0: You become a Monstrosity in addition to any other creature type(s).
Hybrid Wolf Form.entries.1: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you may voluntarily transform into a hybrid half-wolf creature. While in your hybrid wolf form, the following rules apply:
Hybrid Wolf Form.entries.2.items.0: Your Strength score becomes 18 unless it is higher.
Hybrid Wolf Form.entries.2.items.1: Your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases by 10 feet, and you can use the {@action Dash|XPHB} action as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}.
Hybrid Wolf Form.entries.2.items.2: You can't cast spells or concentrate on spells. Your ability to speak is reduced to short, basic, guttural responses.
Hybrid Wolf Form.entries.2.items.3: While transformed, you can use weapons and equipment as normal, unless specified elsewhere.
Hybrid Wolf Form.entries.2.items.4: You gain a Claw or Bite attack that you may use in place of any weapon attack. These attacks deal {@dice 1d8} plus your Strength or Dexterity modifier Slashing damage. You are proficient with this attack, and you add either your Strength or Dexterity modifier to attack and damage rolls. This attack is not an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, nor can it be used in place of an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}.
Hybrid Wolf Form.entries.2.items.5: After taking an {@action Attack|XPHB} action, you may take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} later in that turn to attack once with either your Claw or Bite.
Hybrid Wolf Form.entries.3: Your hybrid wolf form lasts 1 hour per Transformation Stage. If you entered your hybrid form voluntarily, you can revert to your normal form by using a {@action Magic|XPHB} action on your turn. ({@filter Flaws listed below|optionalfeatures|source=grimhollowpg24|feature type=ly:tf|miscellaneous=} may override this rule.)
Hybrid Wolf Form.name: Hybrid Wolf Form
Illusionary Cloak.entries.0: You can use a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to wrap yourself in an illusionary cloak for up to 1 hour. During this time, you can make yourself look like another creature of your general size and humanoid shape. This illusion ends when you use a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to end it, or if you die or gain the {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} condition.
Illusionary Cloak.entries.1: A creature can pierce the illusion by taking the {@action Study|XPHB} action and succeeding on a DC 20 Intelligence ({@skill Investigation|XPHB}) check, thus seeing your true form.
Illusionary Cloak.name: Illusionary Cloak
Impromptu Artisan.entries.0: You've never known the luxury of always having the  gear you need, but you have more than learned to make do. If you possess {@item Artisan's Tools|XPHB} with which you have proficiency, and if you have access to appropriate raw materials and any additional necessary equipment (as the GM determines), you can use a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} to craft any one nonmagical item worth 10 GP or less, including:
Impromptu Artisan.entries.1.items.2: A unique item that performs a simple function approved by the GM
Impromptu Artisan.entries.2: The gear you create is workable but not high quality, and can't be sold except as the GM determines.
Impromptu Artisan.entries.3.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, you can use Impromptu Artisan during a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, during which you craft one nonmagical item worth 50 GP or less.
Impromptu Artisan.entries.3.name: Master Artisan
Impromptu Artisan.name: Impromptu Artisan
Improved Fanged Bite.entries.0: As an {@action Attack|XPHB} action, you can make 2 {@optfeature Fanged Bite|GrimHollowPG24} attacks. Additionally, you can make a {@optfeature Fanged Bite|GrimHollowPG24} attack as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} if you don't make a {@optfeature Fanged Bite|GrimHollowPG24} attack as part of an {@action Attack|XPHB} action on the same turn.
Improved Fanged Bite.name: Improved Fanged Bite
Improved Pact Weapon.entries.0: You can use any weapon you summon with your Pact of the Blade feature as a spellcasting focus for your warlock spells.
Improved Pact Weapon.entries.1: In addition, the weapon gains a +1 bonus to its attack and damage rolls, unless it is a magic weapon that already has a bonus to those rolls.
Improved Pact Weapon.entries.2: Finally, the weapon you conjure can be a {@item shortbow|phb}, {@item longbow|phb}, {@item light crossbow|phb}, or {@item heavy crossbow|phb}.
Improved Pact Weapon.name: Improved Pact Weapon
Improviser.entries.0: When needs demand, you get the job done better than most. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, choose one skill or tool that you don't have proficiency with. You have proficiency in that skill or with that tool for 1 hour. You regain the use of this feature when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Improviser.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on ability checks you make using the skill or tool you select with Improviser. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Improviser.entries.1.name: Expert Improviser
Improviser.name: Improviser
Inborn Perception.entries.0: The best way to avoid danger is to make sure you're the first person to notice it. You have proficiency in the {@skill Perception|XPHB} skill.
Inborn Perception.entries.1.entries.0: If you take this trait twice, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Perception checks. You can use this feature a number of times equal to twice your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, regaining all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Inborn Perception.entries.1.name: Piercing Perception
Inborn Perception.name: Inborn Perception
Incorporeal Movement.entries.0: You are able to loosen the binds that tie you to the material world to move through solid objects and creatures. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can make yourself incorporeal until the start of your next turn. While in this form, you gain the following benefits:
Incorporeal Movement.entries.1.items.0: You can move through solid objects at your full {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}. You take {@damage 1d10} Force damage if you end your turn inside a solid object and are moved to the nearest open space.
Incorporeal Movement.entries.1.items.1: You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to all damage types except Force damage.
Incorporeal Movement.entries.2: You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and regain all uses after finishing a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Incorporeal Movement.name: Incorporeal Movement
Infernal Smite.entries.0: Once on each of your turns, you can choose one creature you have just damaged with a weapon attack, an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, or a cantrip. Deal an extra {@damage 1d6} Acid, Cold, or Fire damage to that creature, using the same damage type you chose for {@optfeature Fiendish Soul|GrimHollowPG24}. You can add this damage a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} plus your Transformation Stage, but no more than once per damage roll. You regain all uses of this ability when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Infernal Smite.entries.1: At higher Transformation Stages, you do an additional {@damage 1d6} of damage per Transformation Stage, for a total of {@dice 2d6} at Stage 2, {@dice 3d6} at Stage 3, and {@dice 4d6} at Stage 4.
Infernal Smite.name: Infernal Smite
